PROCLAMATION
TO THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY :
WHEREAS , it is apparent that the income of the State has
been very materially reduced on account of the decreased valu-
ation of all kinds of property for taxation purposes by the
State , and that a further reduction in the State's already de-
pleted revenues was occasioned by virtue of the passage by
the last Regular Session of the General Assembly of Kentucky
of laws reducing the cost of automobile and truck license
plates , and because of the reduction in the tax rate upon all
real estate within the Commonwealth from thirty cents to five
cents upon the valuation fixed thereon for state taxation pur-
poses ; and ,
WHEREAS , by virtue of the present financial condition of
the country as a whole and under the revenue laws now in
force in this State , the income of the State , for the support of
its general government and to meet the needs of government ,
has been reduced within the last two years from more than
$20,000,000 to less than $ 10,000,000 ; and , therefore , the income
of the State will be utterly inadequate to efficiently maintain
the various departments of the State Government and ade-
quately support the schools of the State commensurate with
their needs ; and ,
WHEREAS , the government of the United States , through
its Congress , has passed laws providing funds to assist the
States of the Nation in relieving the distress of their citizens ,
caused by unemployment , provided the states match the money
so provided , as required by federal authorities ; aud ,
WHEREAS , certain domestic life insurance companies ,
under present laws , are denied the privilege of borrowing
money from the Reconstruction Finance Corporation for the
reason that said companies are not authorized to issue unassess-
able stock that can be placed as collateral to secure loans from
the Federal Government ; and ,
WHEREAS , by reason of the reduction of tax upon real
estate , herein mentioned , many of the sheriffs of the State ,
elected in counties thereof, will have the emoluments of their
offices materially reduced , and to such extent that their income
will be insufficient to justify them in discharging the duties of

viii PROCLAMATION
such offices in the counties thus affected . These officals should ,
by virtue of the provisions of an enabling act , be relieved from
the conditions described ; and ,
WHEREAS , the regular 1934 session of the General As-
sembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky concluded its labors
and adjourned without having enacted an appropriation act,
or without having made any appropriation for the support of
the general State Governnent or any of its departments or
divisions , and so adjourned without providing any additional
revenue to meet the needs of the State Government , notwith-
standing that its depleted condition at the time was well known ,
and failed to enact any revenue measures whatsoever in lieu of
the revenues lost to the State by virtue of certain laws passed
by that session ; and ,
'WHEREAS , certain laws , that were enacted at the ses-
sion referred to , were defective on account of irregularities oc-
curring in the enrolled bill that would have destroyed their
effectiveness . These laws should be re -enacted , repealed or
amended at the ensuing special session :
THEREFORE , in consideration of the facts and conditions
mentioned , and in accordance with the power vested in me by
by the Constitution of the State to convene the General Assem-
bly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in extraordinary session ,
I, as Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , do issue
this , my proclamation , convening the General assembly of
Kentucky in extraordinary session at the seat of government
at Frankfort , Kentucky , at twelve o'clock noon , on Wednesday ,
the ninth day of May , 1934 , for the sole purpose of considering
the following subjects :
1. To enact such measures as will provide sufficient reve-
nue to carry on all proper functions of the State Government ,
including common schools and higher institutions of learning ,
and to provide necessary funds for relief of the unemployed and
distressed of the State ; and to repeal any laws in conflict with
such measures .
2. To appropriate necessary sums to the various State de-
partments , commissions and agencies to carry on their respec-
tive duties and functions .
3. To amend and re -enact or correct Senate Bili No. 330 ,
1934 General Assembly .
4. To amend and re -enact a bill introduced at the 1934
session of the General Assembly, being House Bill No. 360 , re-
lating to the consolidation of the offices of jailers and sheriffs .

PROCLAMATION ix
5. To enact a measure authorizing domestic life insurance
companies to issue non-assessable stock .
6. To amend , re -enact or repeal House Bill No. 781 , known
as the Nudist Bill .
7. To enact a measure compensating the sheriffs of the
State in sums not exceeding the amount lost to them in com-
missions at four per cent by reason of the reduction of the real
estate tax from thirty cents to five cents for State purposes .
8. To enact laws relating to chattel mortgages .
9. To enact a measure relating to the acceptance by the
State of the William Lindsay property , devised to it by Eleanor
Holmes Lindsay .
10. To amend and re -enact House Bill No. 477 , relating to
a refund of auto truck license fees .
You will, therefore , in accordance with this proclamation ,
assemble in said extraordinary session at the seat of govern-
ment at Frankfort , Kentucky , on the day and date above writ-
ten, to consider the subjects herein mentioned , and only said
subjects .
Given under my hand , this the seventh day of May, 1934 ,
and in the one hundred and forty -second year of the Common-
wealth .
BY THE GOVERNOR :
( Signed ) SARA W. MAHAN
Secretary of State
(Signed ) RUBY LAFFOON
Governor of Kentucky

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GEN-
ERAL ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF
FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON WEDNESDAY , MAY THE
NINTH , AND ENDED ON MONDAY , JULY THE SECOND ,
1934 .
CHAPTER I.
AN ACT relating to chattel mortgages and other liens and
pledges of personal property , and providing , but not by way
of limitation , for the validity , execution , delivery, registration ,
priority , release , assignment , satisfaction , and enforcement of
chattel mortgages and /or deeds of trust on personal property
and /or other liens or pledges of personal property and defin-
ing and making mortgageable as personal property , but not by
way of limitation , livestock , poultry , vertebrates , tools ,
machinery , farm implements , emblements , bees , insects , fruits ,
berries , nuts , and all viticultural , horticultural , forestry and
agricultural products whatsoever , and further providing for
fees for recording , releasing , and assigning chattle mortgages
and /or deeds of trust .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any person , firm , corporation , association
or legal entity , whether acting in an individual or
in a fiduciary capacity , unless expressly denied the
privilege by law ( and if a fiduciary with the con-
sent and approval of the proper court ) , may enter
into an agreement with and borrow funds or obtain
credit from a production credit association organ-
ized under the Farm Credit Act of 1933. The Central
Acts- 1

2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and/or any Regional Bank for Cooperatives , a
regional agricultural credit corporation , a Federal
intermediate credit bank ( or any institution which
has made arrangements to discount therewith , or to
procure funds therefrom on the security of , the ob-
ligations of the borrower ) , the Reconstruction
Finance Corporation , the Government of the United
States or any department , agency or officer thereof ,
or from any person , association , partnership or cor-
poration allowed by the law to loan money and/or
extend credit and take security therefor , and may
give as evidence thereof a promissory note or notes
or other legal evidence of indebtedness and may val-
idly secure said note or notes or other legal evidence
of indebtedness and/or any future advances ( as
hereinafter provided ) to be made by the lender
whether to be evidenced by written instrument or
otherwise due from the borrower to the lender , by a
chattel mortgage or chattel deed of trust ( either
with or without a power of sale in the mortgagee , the
trustee named in the chattel deed of trust or in a
third person and/or corporation ) upon personal
property of any kind and/or character including
(but not by way of limitation ) .
a. Any and/or all livestock of every kind and
character whatsoever , including the increase , issue ,
progeny and/or produce thereof , whether such in-
crease , issue , progeny and/or produce is in being or
may come into being during the existence of the
mortgage or any renewal thereof.
b. Any and/or all poultry , including pigeons and
birds , game or otherwise , of every kind and char-
acter whatsoever , including the increase , issue ,
progeny and/or produce thereof, whether such in-
crease , issue , progeny and/or produce is in being or
may come into being during the existence of the
mortgage or any renewal thereof .

CHAPTER 1 3
c. Any and/or all vertebrates having any agri-
cultural or commercial value , including the increase ,
issue , progeny and/or produce thereof, whether such
increase , issue , progeny and/or produce is in being
or may come into being during the existence of the
mortgage or any renewal thereof .
d. Any and/or all tools , machinery, farming im-
plements of any and all kinds and character , and/or
equipment of any kind or character , owned at the
time of the execution of the mortgage and/or there-
after acquired and/or which may be thereafter
acquired .
e . Any and/or all emblements and any and/or
all crops ( either annual or perennial ) whether in-
dustrial , agricultural , field , hothouse , greenhouse
and/or garden crops , either or whether grown or
growing under glass in hothouse , greenhouse or in
field or garden or to be planted within one year from
the date of such mortgage .
f. Any and/or all bees and/or other insects which
in themselves have any agricultural or commercial
value or from which may be derived directly and/or
indirectly in any manner or form whatsoever , any
article or substance having any agricultural and/or
commercial value .
g . Any and/or all fruits and/or berries ( either
annual or perennial ) the product of any tree , vine ,
bush or shrub , either or whether grown or growing
in bud or other form or which may be expected
to mature within one year from the date of such
mortgage .
h . Any and/or all horticultural , viticultural ,
and/or forestry products , fruits , nuts , berries , or
substances , the result either directly or indirectly
of the fertilization of any root , tree , vine , bush ,
shrub, cutting , slip , and/or flower and/or resulting
from the growth of such tree , vine , bush , root , cut-

4++ ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ting , slip , shrub , and/or flower , either or whether
grown or growing in bud or other form or which
may be planted and/or growing in bud or in any
other form or manner whatsoever and which may
be expected to mature within one year from the
date of such mortgage .
i. Any and /or all agricultural products not
otherwise herein provided for of whatsoever nature
which are planted in or on and produced from the
soil , or otherwise , either or whether grown or grow-
ing or to be planted within one year from the date
of such mortgage .
j . Any and/or all things partaking of an agri-
cultural nature or having any agricultural value or
use whatsoever attached to and/or forming part of
the land or soil which may be severed therefrom .
All such property shall be deemed to be personal
property and validly mortgageable as such .
The term " agricultural products " as used in this
Act includes horticultural , viticultural , forestry ,
nut , dairy , livestock , bee and any and all farm prod-
ucts unless the context or subject matter requires
otherwise .
Such mortgage shall be a good and valid lien
upon the property therein described and thereby
conveyed , which lien from the time of the filing of
such mortgage with the recording officer or official
shall be a good and valid lien against , and superior
to all rights of , any and all creditors of the mort-
gagor , and any and all subsequent purchasers , mort-
gagees , lienors , and incumbrancers , including judg-
ment creditors of the mortgagor and any and all
third persons , excepting the landlord's lien shall be
superior to all other liens as provided in Section
2323 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition .
Where the mortgagor is a sharescropper or tenant
for a definite term , for a term running from month

CHAPTER 1 5
to month , for life , for years , at will or by suffrance ,
and the landlord or owner of the premises occupied
by such sharecropper or tenant shall have any right ,
title , claim and/or interest in any such personal
property , a release , waiver or subordination by such
landlord or owner of such right , title , interest and/or
claim to such sharecropper or tenant shall be suf-
ficient to permit such tenant or sharecropper to
validly mortgage such personal property free from
any right , title , interest and/or claim of the land-
lord and/or owner . Such releases may be filed in
the manner herein provided for and when so filed
shall , from the time of filing thereof, be good and
valid as against all persons whomsoever , excepting
the landlord's lien shall be superior to all other
liens as provided by Section 2323 , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , but valid however be-
tween the parties from the time of execution .
§ 2. Any such mortgage may validly secure
future advances to be made by the mortgagee or
lender , at its option , within a period of one year
from the execution of such mortgage , but not to ex-
ceed in the aggregate an amount stated in said
mortgage ; and any and all advances so made shall
be secured by such mortgage equally or as provided
for therein and to the same extent as the amount
originally advanced on the security of such
mortgage and shall be valid as to all persons whom-
soever . A chattel mortgage securing advances to be
made in installments as any crop or mortgaged se-
curity progresses in growth and/or expectation of
value shall be valid and effective in all respects
against the mortgagor , existing or subsequent
creditors of the mortgagor , subsequent purchasers ,
subsequent lienors and/or incumbrancers , and any
and all third persons , including the executors and
administrators of the mortgagor .

6 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 3. The lien of any chattel mortgage which has
been executed and filed for record in accordance
with the terms and provisions of this Act shall be
entitled to priority in all respects over any unre-
corded conditional sales contract and/or any mort-
gage , lien or incumbrance which has not been filed
with the proper recording officer , and shall be valid
and effective in all respects against the mortgagor ,
existing or subsequent creditors of the mortgagor ,
subsequent purchasers , and any and all third per-
sons , including the executors and administrators of
the mortgagor .
§ 4. Such mortgage may validly include replace-
ments of any of the mortgaged property therein de-
scribed and may validly include the increase of
animals and livestock of all kinds and the issue ,
increase , progeny and produce of any and all prop-
erty mortgageable under the terms and provisions
of this Act , including the increase , issue , progeny
and produce of any original increase , issue , and/or
progeny. A mortgage of sheep or goats shall be held
to include the wool or mohair which may be clipped
therefrom . No chattel mortgage of livestock and
hay, grain and/or other food or feed stuffs and/or
property consumable by its use shall be invalid in
any particular because provision is contained there-
in , or the mortgagee consents , that the mortgagor
may use and/or consume food , feed , forage and/or
fodder crops in preserving , maintaining , fattening ,
and/or preparing for market the livestock or other
property covered thereby .
5. Any mortgage executed under and pur-
suant to this Act to the governor of the farm credit
administration of the United States pursuant to ex-
isting or future acts of congress and/or a produc-
tion credit association organized under the farm
credit act of 1933 , the central and/or any regional

CHAPTER 1 7
bank for cooperatives , a federal intermediate credit
bank , the reconstruction finance corporation , the
government of the United States or any department ,
agency , instrumentality or officer thereof , may
validly provide that the mortgagor shall , as the
agent or trustee for the mortgagee or lender or
owner or holder of the secured debt , have the right
to sell or exchange any of the mortgaged chattels
under the conditions stated in said mortgage , if the
proceeds of such sale or exchange are applied upon
the mortgage debt or subjcted to the lien of said
mortgage , or are used solely for the purpose of
paying the expenses of cultivating , harvesting , pre-
paring , for market, processing , marketing , or other-
wise preserving or rendering merchantable or sal-
able the remaining property covered by said mort-
gage , and such provision shall not in any way render
invalid the lien of said mortgage or its preference
or priority as herein stated . Said mortgage may
validly provide that purchasers need not be required
to see as to the proper application of the proceeds
of the sale . Any such sales or exchange may be
made in accordance with such provisions without
notice to or consent of any person claiming any right
in or to the mortgaged property , and such property
received in exchange shall be as validly covered by
the mortgage as the original property .
§ 6. Any chattel mortgage given under and pur-
suant to this Act may be executed by the mortgagor
or by his duly authorized agent and need not be
sealed , but shall be witnessed and/or acknowledged
as now or hereafter required by law for the witness-
ing or acknowledging of deeds conveying real
property .
§ 7. It shall not be necessary to the validity of
any chattel mortgage given under and pursuant to
this Act for any party thereto to make or record

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
•any affidavit , affirmation , statement of truth or
correctness of claim or oath as to the consideration
of such chattel mortgage , the validity of the debt
secured or any other matter in connection there-
with , and consideration for the execution of such
mortgage shall be presumed as between the parties
thereto and all other persons .
$ 8. A. Any chattel mortgage or instrument
intended to operate as such , shall be filed in the
county where the mortgagor , if a resident of the
state , resides at the time of the execution thereof ;
or, if the mortgagor is not a resident of the state , in
the county where the mortgaged property is located
at the time of the execution of the mortgage . In the
case of a mortgage executed by more than one mort-
gagor, the filing shall be in each county where the
resident mortgagor or mortgagors reside ; and also ,
if any such mortgagor is not a resident of the state ,
then in the county where the mortgaged property is
located at the time of the execution of the mortgage .
It shall not be necessary to record chattel mort-
gages , deeds of trust , or other instruments intended
to operate as pledges , mortgages or liens upon per-
sonal property , and such instruments shall not be
recorded at length , but , upon filing such instruments
in the office of the County Court Clerk , all persons
shall thereby be charged with notice thereon and of
the rights of the mortgagee , trustee , his or its as-
signee or representative thereunder .
The County Court Clerk shall keep a separate
book called a " chattel mortgage minute book " in
which shall be entered an abstract of all such instru-
ments intended to operate as pledges , mortgages or
liens upon personal property , which book shall be
ruled off in separate columns , with heads as fol-
lows : Time of filing , name of mortgagor , name of
mortgagee or trustee , date of instrument , amount

CHAPTER 1 9
secured thereby , when due , property mortgaged or
pledged , and remarks ; and the proper entry shall be
made , immediately upon the filing of the instrument ,
under each of said heads .
Under the head of " property mortgaged " it shall
be sufficient to enter a general description of the
property , and an index shall be kept in the manner
as required for pledges and mortgages of real
estate .
The County Court Clerk shall mark the original
instrument filed , with the time thereof and the
number and page of the book where the abstract
thereof may be found , and said instrument shall be
preserved as a public record of his office , filed and
indexed in regular alphabetical order so as to be
readily accessible for inspection .
When the debt secured by any such instrument
has been paid or satisfied , or any such debt and the
lien is assigned or transferred by marginal notation
by the mortgagee , trustee , or the owner thereof ,
such satisfaction , assignment , or transfer shall be
entered under the heading " remarks " in the " chat-
tel mortgage minute book " , signed by the mort-
gagee , trustee , or owner thereof and attested by the
County Court Clerk , or such release , payment , satis-
faction , assignment or transfer may be by a separate
instrument duly acknowledged and filed in the of-
fice of the County Court Clerk , in which latter case
the County Court Clerk shall note , over his attesta-
tion , under the heading " remarks " the satisfaction
or the assignment of the debt and lien , and which
separate instrument shall be filed , indexed , and ab-
stracted in the same manner as the original mort-
gage .
When such mortgages or pledges are fully satis-
fied and paid , the County Court Clerk shall de-
liver the original instrument and any other instru-
8

10 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ment filed , acknowledging full satisfaction thereof ,
to the mortgagor or his assigns . Such delivery may
be made by placing such instrument in United States
mail , postgage prepaid , directed to the last known
address of the mortgagor or his assigns or repre-
sentatives .
In cases where the mortgage or debt is assigned
or transferred by separate instrument , the County
Court Clerk shall file , index , abstract , and preserve
such instruments or transfers as well as the original
mortgage or pledge , until the debt is fully satisfied .
A certified copy of any such instrument filed , or
notation or abstract made under the provision of
this Act , certified under the hand and seal if any ,
of the County Court Clerk , shall be admitted in evi-
dence as prima facie proof of its genuineness , with
the same effect as the original instrument or chattel
mortgage minute book might have if introduced by
the County Court Clerk or custodian thereof , but
subject to be rebutted by competent and relevant
testimony .
At the time of filing of each such instrument the
recording officer shall, upon request , issue to the
person filing the same a receipt in writing which
shall contain the names of the parties to the mort-
gage , its date , amount , number and the time of
filing thereof .
B. Any release , waiver or subordination given
under and pursuant to Section 1 of this Act , or in-
strument intended to operate as such release , or
agreement for the subordination of prior liens re-
specting property covered by any chattel mortgage
executed under the provisions of this Act , or instru-
ment affecting in any way any right , title , interest
and /or claim to the mortgaged property may be
filed with the County Court Clerk in the same man-
ner as herein provided for the filing of chattel mort-

L 11CHAPTER 1
gages ; and when so filed shall be indexed by him
in the same manner and in the same books as herein
provided for the indexing of chattel mortgages and
the County Court Clerk shall add to such entries
of each said instrument a marginal notation iden-
tifying such instrument as a " Release " or " Sub-
ordination " as the case may be.
C. Every duly acknowledged deed of assign-
ment made by any instrument executed under and
pursuant to this Act shall be filed in the office of
the County Court Clerk where the original instru-
ment is filed , and shall be abstracted by the County
Court Clerk in the " chattel mortgage minute book "
in the column headed " remarks " . The mortgagee
may also assign by marginal notation entered in the
" remarks " column of the " chattel mortgage minute
book " which notation shall state the date , and the
name of the assignee and shall be signed by the mort-
gagee ( or assignor ) and shall be attested by the
County Court Clerk .
§ 10. A chattel mortgage executed under and
pursuant to this Act shall be invalid as against cred-
itors , purchasers , junior mortgagees , other lienors
and encumbrancers and third parties after the ex-
piration of a period of three years , reckoning from
the time of the filing of said chattel mortgage or
from the time of filing of a statement as herein pro-
vided for , unless before the expiration of such term
the mortgagee or some one in its behalf shall file a
statement containing the names of the parties to
the mortgage , the time and place where filed , and
the amount then due thereon for principal and in-
terest , and which case the lien of the mortgage shall
be extended for three years from and after the date
of the filing of said statement . The officer filing
the statement shall be entitled to the same fee as in
the case of the filing and entry of a chattel mort-

12 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
gage , and he shall likewise enter it in his " chattel
mortgage minute book " .
§ 11. Any mortgage executed under and pur-
suant to this Act may be assigned by an instrument
in writing or endorsement on the original mortgage
signed by the mortgagee , his agent , or assignee or
the owner and /or holder of the secured debt and
filed in the same office as the original mortgage and
such assignment shall, as to all third parties , take
effect and be valid only from the time of filing the
same .
§ 12. Any chattel mortgage executed under and
pursuant to this Act may be discharged by an instru-
ment in writing signed by an officer or authorized
agent of the mortgagee or his assignee . Any duly
acknowledged deed of release or discharge executed
under and pursuant to this Act of any instrument
provided for herein shall be delivered to the mort-
gagor and filed by him with the County Court Clerk
in the same manner as herein provided for the filing
of chattel mortgages and when so filed shall be in-
dexed by the recording officer in the same manner
and in the same book as herein provided for the in-
dexing of chattel mortgages and the recording officer
shall add to such entries of each discharge so in-
dexed a marginal notation identifying such instru-
ment as a " discharge " or " satisfaction " . The
mortgagee may also release by marginal notation
entered in the " remarks " column of the " chattel
mortgage minute book " which notation shall show
the date thereof , shall be signed by the mortgagee
( or assignee ) and shall be attested by the County
Court Clerk .
§ 13. The lien of the mortgage shall not be ex-
tinguished or impaired by the removal of the mort-
gaged property from the county in which the mort-
gage is filed . The mortgagee or any assignee of

CHAPTER 1 13
the mortgage and/or the owner and/or holder of
the secured debt may expressly consent to the ex-
tinguishment of the lien as to all or any part of the
mortgaged property and may condition such con-
sent in any manner , which conditions shall be bind-
ing upon any purchaser from the mortgagor and any
and all third persons .
The mortgagee or any assignee of the mortgage
and/or the owner and/or holder of the secured debt
may release the lien of the mortgage or trust deed
as to any of the mortgaged property without any
impairment of his right or lien as to the remainder
of the mortgaged property , which shall stand as se-
curity for the remaining unpaid indebtedness free
from any right and/or claim of any third person for
a marshaling of security ..
Any chattel mortgage executed under and pur-
suant to this Act may validly provide that any part
of the security may be released from time to time
upon the payment of a specified sum or sums and
the mortgagor and all third parties shall be bound
thereby and take subject to such right of the mort-
gagee to make such partial releases without impair-
ment of the mortgage lien .
§ 14. In addition to any other methods of fore-
closing chattel mortgages or deeds of trust under the
laws of this State , whether pursuant to a power of
sale contained in the mortgage or otherwise , chatteľ
mortgages executed under and pursuant to this
Act to secure the governor of the farm credit admin-
istration of the United States pursuant to existing
or future acts of congress , and /or a production
credit association organized under the farm credit
act of 1933 , the central and/or any regional bank
for co -operatives , a federal intermediate credit bank ,
the reconstruction finance corporation , the govern-
ment of the United States or any department ,

foreclosed in the manner hereinafterhereinafter provided .

gagee ) ." A copy of such notice of sale must be

The proceeds of such sale shall be applied to the ex-

the sale are not sufficient to pay said claims , the
mortgagee may recover the deficiency from the
mortgagor or from anyone who has assumed the ob-
ligation of the mortgagor with respect to the mort-
gage . Neither the bringing of an action by the mort-
gagee to recover the whole or any part of said
claims nor the recovery of a judgment thereon nor
the collection of a portion of said claims shall be in-
consistent with or prevent in any way the fore-
closure of said mortgage as herein provided . The
mortgagee or trustee may act by agent or attorney ,
and need not be present in person at the sale .
§ 15. This Act shall be liberally construed to
effectuate the purposes hereof and substantial com-
pliance herewith shall be sufficient hereunder . The
provisions of this Act , so far as the same are appli-
cable , shall govern and control chattel mortgages

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
given under and pursuant hereto . If any provision
of this Act , or the application thereof to any person
or circumstances , is held invalid , the remainder of
the Act , and the application of such provisions to
other persons or circumstances , shall not be affected
thereby .
$ 16. All Acts and parts of Acts and statutory
provisions and all laws or parts of laws in conflict
with the provisions of this Act or any part of this
Act are hereby expressly repealed .
§ 17. This Act may be cited as the Chattel Mort-
gage Act of 1934 .
§ 18. The term " mortgage " where used in
this Act shall include " deed of trust " or any instru-
ment given to secure any present and/or future in-
debtedness .
The term " borrower " where used in this Act
shall include the original borrower , his administra-
tors , executors , representatives , heirs , distributees ,
devisees , legatees , successors and/or assigns .
The term " lender " where used in this Act shall
include the original lender, its successors , trans-
ferees , indorsees , and/or assigns and/or the owner
and/or holder of the secured indebtedness .
The term " mortgagee " where used in this Act
shall include the original mortgagee and the owner
and/or holder of the secured indebtedness , his ad-
ministrators , executors , representatives , heirs , dis-
tributees , devisees , legatees , transferees , indorsees
and/or assigns .
The term " mortgagor " where used in this Act
shall include the original mortgagor , his administra-
tors , executors , representatives , heirs , distributees ,
devisees , legatees , successors and/or assigns .
Any term used in the singular may as required by
the context , be read as plural and vice versa .

CHAPTER 2 17
$ 19. Because of the fact that a serious economic
depression has adversely affected thousands of citi-
zens of Kentucky , and because of the fact that great
numbers of farmers in this state are in distressed
financial conditions , and because of the fact that it
is urgent that a great many of these farmers borrow
money immediately , and because of the fact that cer-
tain agencies of the Federal Government and units
or divisions of the Federal Farm Credit Administra-
tion are now ready , able , willing and anxious to be
of assistance to those farmers by making loans to
said farmers and take as security therefor chattel
mortgages on the subjects mentioned in this Act ,
and because of the fact that the present chattel mort-
gage law in the state of Kentucky does not offer
ample security to the said agencies of the Federal
Farm Credit Administration and prospective lend-
ers to induce them to loan moneys on such security ,
an emergency is hereby declared to exist and this
Act shall be effective immediately upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Approved June 25 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 2 101
Sec . 12 , of Article 3, Chapter 25 , of the Acts of 1934 , provides
that in the event that the General Assembly shall fail to make
an appropriation for any fiscal year for any purpose , or purposes ,
required to be executed by provisions of existing laws , or the
Governor shall veto an appropriation essential to the carrying
out of any such purpose , or purposes , then , and in that event , an
appropriation equal in amount to the appropriation made or
deemed to have been made under the provisions of this section
for such purpose , or purposes , for the fiscal year next preceding ,
exclusive of any such appropriation for extraordinary expenses
and capital outlays , shall be deemed to have been made and
shall continue available from year to year until an appropria-
tion shall have been made as provided in this act and shall be-
come effective .
Under the provisions of this Section the Department of Health
is hereby deemed to have been made an appropriation equal in
amount to operating receipts for each and every bureau and
agency provided for under sub -section L- 1 and u-1, inclusive , and
sub -sections L-2 to u-2, inclusive , of Sec . 18-A, of House Bill No.
67 , in Senate .
In vetoing sub -sections L -1 to u -1, inclusive , and sub -sections
L-2 to u-2, inclusive , Sec . 18 -A , of this Act , it is my purpose to
permit the said bureaus and agencies of the Department of
Health to operate under the provisions of Sec . 12 and 14 , of
Article 3 , Chapter 25 , Acts of 1934. (Regular Session ) .
Sec . 14 , of Article 3 , Chapter 25 , Acts of 1934 , ( Regular Ses-
sion ) provides for the establishment , by appropriation , of re-
volving fund accounts in the State Treasury to be composed of
operating receipts of industries , farms, hospitals , sanitoria ,
dining halls , dormatories , book stores , refreshment booths , ath-
letics or activities , self-supporting in whole or in part and /or
by depositing the operating receipts of such activities and agen-
cies in the State Treasury to the credit of such revolving ac-
count . The activities and services listed in this section appear
to be sufficiently comprehensive to include the services rendered
by the bureaus named under sub -section L-1 to u -1, inclusive , of
Sec . 18 -A, of House Bill No. 67, in Senate , which sub -sections I
have herein vetoed in toto .
The Department of Health may , through depositing operating
receipts from such bureaus in the State Treasury to the credit
of a revolving fund or revolving funds , withdraw the same for
the use and benefit of such bureaus .

102 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 63 , names the bureaus and agencies of the Department
of Health for which appropriations are herein vetoed along with
certain other bureaus , boards , and agencies .
Specific provision is made in connection with each bureau ,
board or agency for the handling of operating receipts for each
of the agencies listed in this section not affected by this veto .
The veto of this section is made necessary in order that the
Department of Health may be permitted to operate under the
provisions of Secs . 12 and 14 of Article 3 , Chapter 25 , Acts of
1934 .
The provisions of this bill and the appropriations made there-
under , with the exception of those herein specifically referred
to , is hereby approved .
This July 6 , 1934 . RUBY LAFFOON , Governor .
CHAPTER 3.
AN ACT, providing for an annual appropriation of five hundred
thousand dollars for the purchase and distribution of text
books and giving the State Board of Education authority to
administer textbook laws and to adopt such rules and regula-
tions as may be found necessary to carry the textbook laws
into effect ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the State Board of Education shall have full
authority to purchase text books as provided by
Chapter forty- eight , Acts of nineteen hundred
twenty-eight , and shall have full authority to adopt
such rules and regulations for the purchase and
distribution of such books as in its judgment may be
necessary , provided such rules and regulations are
not in conflict with law . In the purchase and distri-
bution of text books the State Board of Education
shall begin with the first grade and shall furnish
textbooks to the first grade before any are bought
for the second grade. After books have been fur-
nished to the first grade, if additional funds are
available the State Board shall furnish books to the

CHAPTER 3 103
second grade, and so on , until the fund is exhausted ;
provided if, in the judgment of the State Board of
Education sufficient funds are not available to
furnish all the text books to any grade , then that
Board shall have authority to determine for what
subject or subjects in that particular grade text
books shall be provided . There is hereby , appropri-
ated annually , out of the General Fund , the sum of
five hundred thousand dollars for the purchase and
distribution of text books as provided in this Act,
and any unused balance left from one year shall be
carried to the following year and shall be used for
the purposes provided in this Act . This sum is irre-
vocably set aside for purchase and distribution of
text books as provided in this Act . The State Board
of Education is hereby given full authority to ad-
minister the purchase and distribution of text books
in the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and shall ex-
pend from the funds appropriated by this Act a suf-
ficient amount to bear the expense of Acts providing
for the purchase and distribution of text books in
this Commonwealth , provided that no amount shall
be expended out of this fund except on proper ap-
proval of the State Board of Education , and by
requisition of the Superintendent of Public Instruc-
tion drawn upon the Auditor of Public Accounts . As
the time is drawing so near for many rural schools
to open , there is hereby declared an emergency to
exist and this bill shall become effective immediately
upon the passage of the two houses of the General
Assembly and the signature of the Governor .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
JAMES H. THOMPSON ,
Presiding officer of the Senate .
Approved July 3, 1934 .
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .

the assistant clerk of the house of representatives
as their respective proportions of this appropria-
tion .
All Acts or parts of Acts in conflict herewith to
the extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .
Whereas , The General Assembly will within a

ment will depart to various sections of the state , an

shall be effective from and after its passage and ap-
proved by the Governor .

chase of State supplies ; disposing of revenue raised under
this act ; providing severable enactment of the sections and
provisions of this act ; declaring an emergency to exist ; re-
pealing all laws or parts of laws , or acts or parts of acts in
conflict herewith to the extent of such conflict only.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions . The meaning and scope of cer-
tain terms used in this act are defined as follows :
"Department of Public Welfare " shall mean the
members of the governing board or department now
in charge of the state prisons and state prisoners ,
or that may hereafter be in such charge by change of
law ; " State Departments " shall include each and
every board , department or agency of the govern-
ment of the state of Kentucky , or any board , depart-
ment or separate agency functioning thereunder ;
" State supplies " shall include all articles or sup-
plies required to be purchased from time to time by
each and every state department ; " state prisons "

108 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall mean the state reformatories or penitentiaries
at Frankfort , Ky . , and Eddyville , Ky . , and the
School of Reform at Greendale , Ky . , and any other
similar institution now existing or that may be
created by law hereafter ; " state prisoners ' shall
include the inmates of all state prisons as herein de-
fined ; " prison labor " shall mean the labor or work
or services of state prisoners as described herein ;
"purchasing agents ' , as herein used , means any
person , board, corporation or institution clothed
with authority to contract for state supplies .
§ 2. The purpose of this Act is hereby declared
to be the raising of revenue or funds through the
employment of prison labor to defray the expenses
of the state prisons and state prisoners and for such
purpose to sell the products of said prison labor to
the state departments for their several needs .
§ 3. The Department of Public Welfare shall be
charged with the duty of providing employment for
all state prisoners , and it shall be the duty of said
department to exhaust every resource at its com-
mand to provide employment for all prisoners in its
custody , and thereby to raise revenue for the self-
support of said prisoners and the state prisons and
to prevent idleness , mutiny and discontent therein .
The Department of Public Welfare shall at once
lease or purchase , or both , all necessary equipment
and materials and have manufactured all clothing—
except that privately furnished , that may be needed
from time to time for the usual , customary and sui-
table clothing of all state prisoners , and of all
inmates and/or patients in all state institutions , who
are required to be clothed by the state ; and there-
after as speedily as possible and in the most practi-
cable way the said department of public welfare
shall lease or purchase , or both , equipment for the

CHAPTER 5 . 109
manufacture and production of all other state sup-
plies , that in the reasonable discretion of said de-
partment may be deemed practicable and/or for
state profit or revenue , and have same manufactured
produced , sold and distributed as provided herein.
In order to better carry out the provisions and
purposes of this act and for the benefit of the state
prisoners the Department of Public Welfare shall
forthwith establish industrial training in the several
state prisons , and shall classify state prisoners ac-
cording to the several uses for prison labor and
ability and talent of the prisoners , utilizing the best
available local talent and such other talent as may
be obtained at an economical and practicable cost ,
and shall select a suitable number of guards and
attendants with such qualifications , wherever possi-
ble , and thereby avoid duplicate expense .
§ 4. In order to effect the purposes of this act
without delay the sum of fifty thousand dollars is
hereby appropriated and set aside out of the Gen-
eral fund as a " Prison Revolving Fund " , which is
hereby created , and same shall be kept as a separate
fund in the state treasury and classified under the
Department of Public Welfare , and all monies col-
lected by operation of this act shall be credited to
said fund , and accumulations to the credit of said
prison revolving fund , which are not necessary
for the operation of the provisions of this act , shall
be used in defraying the expenses of the State pris-
oners and state prisons , and any excess shall be ex-
pended for the benefit of other state institutions as
the department of public welfare may determine .
The money appropriated in this bill shall be ex-
pended by the department of public welfare in its
discretion for the purpose of carrying out the pro-

110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
visions of this Act , and subject to the approval of
the department of public property .
useThe department of public welfare may
any other funds appropriated to it , that may be nec-
essary for the execution of the provisions of this
Act .
§ 5. The department of public welfare shall
sell all products of prison labor to state departments
and as state supplies only, and shall charge therefor
cost of such products , which shall include the cost of
all materials , fifteen cents per hour for prison labor ,
plus twenty per centum thereon for depreciation of
equipment .
It shall be the duty of all state departments to
first seek their state supplies through the depart-
ment of public welfare and if the needed supplies are
there available they shall not purchase any of said
state supplies elsewhere , and the orders for such
state supplies shall be placed as far in advance of
the delivery date as possible by the several purchas-
ing agents and not less than sixty days except in
case of emergency , and all said supplies shall be
paid for out of funds provided each state depart-
ment for such purchase of supplies .
§ 6. It is the purpose of the General Assembly
to , and the sections and provisions of this act are
severable and separately enacted and are declared
to be separate and independent of every other sec-
tion or provision thereof , and if any section or pro-
vision of this act shall be held unconstitutional or
invalid , no other section or provision shall be af-
fected thereby .
§ 7. All laws or parts of laws , or acts or parts
of acts in conflict herewith are hereby repealed to
extent of conflict only.

CHAPTER 6 111
1§ 8. It appearing that nearly all state prisoners
are now idle , numbering approximately 3,000 , and
that due to said lack of employment there is great
danger of mutiny and discontent , and said prisoners
are being maintained at a great cost and loss to the
state of Kentucky , wherefore , an emergency is de-
clared to exist and this act shall take effect imme-
diately upon its passage and approval by the Gov- .
ernor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 6.
AN ACT , to appropriate the sum of Twenty -two thousand five
hundred ( $22,500.00 ) dollars ) to the State Park Commission
for the purpose of erecting buildings and making improve-
ments to the Blue Licks Battlefield State Park , and repealing
all conflicting laws .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated to the Ken-
tucky State Park Commission out of the general ex-
penditure fund the sum of twenty-two thousand five
hundred dollars ( $ 22,500.00 ) , to be used by said
State Park Commission in enlarging the museum
building at the Blue Lick State Park ; in erecting a
residence for the custodian of said Blue Lick State
Park ; for providing for a bird sanctuary , bird baths ,
bird boxes , proper food ; for the improvement , con-
struction and maintenance of roads and driveways ;
and for beautifying said Park . The improvement to
the museum shall be such as to make said museum
fire-proof and as near as practicable theft -proof .
The building to be erected for the custodian shall be
in keeping with the modern ideas for such buildings
and shall be as near as practicable fire-proof .

112 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 2. The State Park Commission as near as prac-
ticable shall after the effective date of this Act start
the buildings and improvements referred to in Sec-
tion one, and shall complete same before January
first , nineteen hundred and thirty -five .
$ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed to the extent of such con-
flicts .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

WHEREAS , in the year 1830 , an oil well was drilled in Cum-
berland County , Kentucky , from which crude oil flowed , at that

One Dollar and Twenty -five Cents ( $1.25 ) per gallon, since

well , not only in Kentucky , but in the United States of
America ; Therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the sum of One Hundred and Fifty ( $ 150.00 )
Dollars be appropriated out of the general fund not
otherwise appropriated for the purpose of purchas-
ing and erecting a permanent marker at said well
in Cumberland County, Kentucky , and Mrs. C. M.
McGee , of Burkesville , Cumberland County, Ken-
tucky , be , and she is hereby designated as the Agent
of the State of Kentucky to purchase and erect said
monument and to draw upon the Treasurer of Ken-
