CHAPTER 13.
AN ACT relating to Revenue and Taxation and providing for
the extension of teachers ' certificates in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky ; the length of time and by whom extended ;
providing for the payment of fee in connection with the ex-
tension of same ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That certificates issued to any teacher , which
certificate expires in the year 1934 , shall be extended

CHAPTER 14 119
one year beyond its expiration ; and such extension
shall be made or issued by the department of certifi-
cation of the State Department of Education upon
application from the teacher , together with a signed
statement from the superintendent of the county or
city in which said teacher making such application
taught, certifying that said teacher had taught either
in the school years 1932-1933 or the school years
1933-1934 ; provided , however , such application shall
be accompanied by a fee of Two Dollars ( $ 2.00 ) , to
be collected by the Department of Education and de-
livered to the State Treasurer to be credited to the
General Expenditure Fund .
Any teacher who has taught for more than twenty
years prior to the effective date of this Act, shall
have a certificate issued for life .
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
§ 2. Whereas , certificates issued to many teach-
ers have expired during the year 1934 ; and
Whereas , the public schools of the State will open
during the months of July and August , an emergency
is declared to exist and this Act will become effective
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 14 .
AN ACT to authorize boards of education of county or inde-
pendent school district located outside of the incorporated
cities of the county to convey real estate for school sites , to
such counties and to lease from such counties elementary
and/or high school buildings at a rental which will amortize
the cost of the erection of such school buildings on such site
for the purpose of qualifying for financial assistance from the
Federal Government , and declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , Many of the school districts of the State of Ken-
tucky are without proper and suitable school buildings and

120 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
other physical facilities thus endangering the health , safety ,
convenience and general welfare of the children thereof ; and ,
WHEREAS , The existing condition of financial distress of such
school boards makes it impossible for them to obtain such
needed physical facilities without supplementary financial as-
sistance ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Federal Government through the Public
Works Administration of the National Industrial Recovery
Act is offering financial aid to the extent of a direct gift of
30 per cent of the cost of labor and materials and a loan of
the remaining 70 per cent of the cost of labor and materials ,
at 4 per cent . interest , necessary for the erection of needed
public buildings for the use and benefit of society ; and ,
WHEREAS , There are no statutory provisions whereby boards
of education of the various school districts of the State of
Kentucky may qualify for financial assistance under regula-
tions for such grants and aids as is prescribed by the Fed-
eral Emergency Administration of Public Works under the
National Industrial Recovery Act ; and ,
WHEREAS , The construction of adequate school buildings for
the children of the various school districts of the state is a
part of the National Recovery Program and is to provide for
the immediate employment of labor and the improvement of
the school property of the State of Kentucky ; Now , therefore,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That for the purpose of providing buildings
for elementary and/or high school purposes , boards
of education of county and independent school dis-
tricts located within counties , exclusive of incorpo-
rated cities of such counties , are hereby authorized
and empowered to convey a fee simple title with
covenant of general warranty of title , to a site for
any such building now held or hereafter acquired
by such boards of education , to such counties .
§ 2. Every such county to which a building site
for a building has been conveyed , as provided in
section one hereof, shall enter into a contract or con-
tracts with some person or persons , corporation or
corporations , for the erection on said site of a build-

CHAPTER 14 121
ing with the necessary appurtenances according to
plans and specifications adopted by such county and
approved by the board of education of the county
or independent school district in which such building
is proposed to be constructed and by the Superin-
tendent of Public Instruction .
§ 3. Immediately upon the approval of the plans
and specifications as provided in section two hereof ,
such board of education shall offer to lease such
building for a term of one or more years from the
time such building shall be completed and ready for
occupancy , which term shall be for a number of years
acceptable to such county . The said lease by its
terms shall give such lessee the right and option to
extend the term of such lease for a term of one or
more years from the expiration of the original term
of such lease and for one or more years from the ex-
piration of each extended term of such lease , until
the original term of such lease shall have been ex-
tended for a total number of years , not exceeding
thirty years , at a rental which , if paid for the origi-
nal term and for each of the full number of years
for which the term of said lease may be extended ,
will amortize the total cost of the erection of said
building and appurtenances , provide an adequate
maintenance fund and in addition thereto a sum
sufficient to pay the cost of insuring the building
against loss or damage by fire and windstorm or
other calamity in such sum as may be agreed by
the parties thereto .
4. This act shall be deemed to create an addi-
tional and alternate method for the acquisition of
elementary and/ or high school buildings by any
county board of education or board of education of
independent school districts and shall not be deemed
to include , alter , amend or repeal any other statute
and shall apply solely to school buildings construct-

122 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ed with financial assistance from the Federal Gov-
ernment .
5. Whereas , many of the school districts to
which this act is applicable are now without ade-
quate school buildings , thus endangering the health ,
safety, convenience and general welfare of the stu-
dents thereof, now, therefore , an emergency is here-
by declared to exist , and this act shall take effect im-
mediately upon its passage and approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 15 .
AN ACT authorizing counties to acquire elementary and /or high
school buildings and to issue bonds therefor, payable from
the revenues of such buildings, for the purpose of qualifying
for financial assistance from the Federal Government , and
declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , Many of the school districts of the State of Ken-
tucky are without proper and suitable school buildings and
other physical facilities thus endangering the health , safety ,
convenience and general welfare of the children thereof ; and ,
WHEREAS , The existing condition of financial distress of such
school boards makes it impossible for them to obtain such
needed physical facilities without supplementary financial as-
sistance ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Federal Government through the Public Works
Administration of the National Industrial Recovery Act is
offering financial aid to the extent of a direct gift of 30 per
cent of the cost of labor and materials and a loan of the
remaining 70 per cent of the cost of labor and materials , at
4 per cent interest, necessary for the erection of needed pub-
lic buildings for the use and benefit of society ; and ,
WHEREAS , There are no statutory provisions whereby boards
of education of the various school districts of the State of
Kentucky may qualify for financial assistance under regula-
tions for such grants and aids as is prescribed by the Federal
Emergency Administration of Public Works under the
National Industrial Recovery Act ; and ,
WHEREAS , The construction of adequate school buildings for
the children of the various school districts of the State is a

CHAPTER 15 123
part of the National Recovery Program and is to provide for
the immediate employment of labor and the improvement of
the school property of the State of Kentucky ; Now , therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That counties whether organized under the
general law or special charter law are hereby author-
ized and empowered to establish and erect ele-
mentary and/or high school buildings , together with
necessary appurtenances thereto under the provi-
sions of this act for the purpose of supplying the
board of education of such county or independent
school districts located outside of the incorporated
cities of such county , adequate buildings necessary
to carry out their corporate duties and powers .
§ 2. For the purpose of defraying the cost of
acquiring any such elementary and/or high school
building by construction and appurtenances thereto ,
any such county maymay borrow money from
the Public Works Administration or other
agency of the Federal Government and issue
negotiable bonds , provided no such bonds
shall be issued unless andand until authorized
by a resolution specifying the proposed under-
taking , the amount of bonds to be issued and the
maximum rate of interest such bonds are to bear ,
which shall not be more than six per cent (6% ) per
annum . Such resolution shall further provide that
the proposed elementary and/or high school build-
ings and appurtenances thereto are to be construct-
ed or acquired pursuant to the provisions of this
act .
$ 3. All bonds under the provisions of this act
may be issued bearing interest at a rate not exceed-
ing six per cent per annum , payable semi-annually ,
and shall be executed in such manner and be payable
at such times , not exceeding thirty years from date
J

124 ACTS OF THE GENERAL Assembly
thereof, and at such place or places as such fiscal
court or board of commissioners shall determine .
§ 4. Any and all bonds shall have and are hereby
declared to have in the hands of bona fide holders
all of the qualities of negotiable instruments under
the law merchant , and shall not be subject to taxa-
tion . In case any of the officers whose signa-
tures or counter signatures appearing on the
bonds or coupons shall cease to be such of-
ficers before the delivery of such bonds , such
signatures or counter signatures shall nevertheless
be valid and sufficient for all purposes the same as
if they had remained in office until such delivery .
Such bonds shall be sold in such manner and upon
such terms as the fiscal court or board of commis-
sioners shall deem for the best interest of such
county . Such bonds when issued shall be payable
solely from the revenue funds derived from such
elementary and/or high school building as provided
in sections eight and nine hereof and shall not con-
stitute an indebtedness of such county within the
meaning of the constitutional provisions or limita-
tions . It shall be plainly stated on the face of such
bond that same has been issued under the provisions
of this act and that it does not constitute an indebted-
ness of such county within the meaning of any con-
stitutional provisions or limitations .
§ 5. All money received from any bonds issued
pursuant hereto shall be applied solely for the es-
tablishment or erection of such elementary and/or
high school building and necessary appurtenances
thereto , provided such moneys may be used also to
advance the payment of the interest on bonds during
the first three years following the date of such bonds .
And there shall be and there is hereby created a
statutory mortgage lien upon the elementary and/ or
high school building and appurtenances so acquired

CHAPTER 15 125
to and in favor of the holders of said bonds and
each of them and to and in favor of the holders of
the coupons of said bonds .
§ 6. The elementary and/or high school buildings
so acquired , together with the appurtenances there-
tɔ , shall remain subject to such statutory lien until
the payment in full of the principal and interest of
the bonds . Any holder of said bonds or of any of
the coupons may , either at law or in equity , protect
and enforce the statutory mortgage lien hereby con-
ferred and may by suit , action , mandamus or other
proceedings , enforce and compel performances of
all duties required by this act , including the making
and collecting of sufficient rates , the segregation of
the income and revenue , and the application thereof.
§ 7. If there be any default in the payment of
the principal or interest of any of said bonds , any
court having jurisdiction of the action may appoint
a receiver to administer said elementary and/or high
school building on behalf of the county , with power
to charge and collect rentals sufficient to provide for
the payment of any bonds or obligations outstand-
ing against said elementary and/or high school build-
ing and for the payment of the operating expenses
and apply the income and revenues in conformity
with this act , and the resolution referred to in sec-
tions eight and nine hereof .
§ 8. When any such county shall desire to con-
struct such elementary and/or high school building ,
the fiscal court or board of commissioners of such
county shall, by resolution , cause plans and specifica-
tions of such building to be constructed , to be duly
made and filed in the office of the county court clerk,
which plans and specifications shall give a full de-
scription of the building to be constructed , the de-
tails thereof and the manner of construction .

126 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The plans and specifications of such building
shall be prepared by an architect selected by the
fiscal court and approved by the board of education
of the school district and shall be submitted to the
board of education of the school district in which
said building is proposed to be constructed and to
the Superintendent of Public Instruction for ap-
proval and if approved , and if such board of educa-
tion shall offer to lease such building for a term of
one or more years from the time such building shall
be completed and ready for occupancy , with the
right and option in such lessee to extend the term
of such lease for a term of one or more years from
the expiration of the original term of such lease and
for one or more years from the expiration of each
extended term of such lease , until the original term
of such lease shall have been extended for a total
number of years , not exceeding thirty years , at a
rental which , if paid for the original term and
for each of the full number of years for which
the term of said lease may be extended , will
amortize the total cost of the erection of said
building and appurtenances , provide an adequate
maintenance fund and in addition thereto a sum
sufficient to pay the cost of insuring the build-
ing erected against loss or damage by fire
and windstorm or other calamity in such sum as
may be agreed by the parties thereof , the fiscal
court or board of commissioners shall by resolution
authorize the issuance of bonds , in the manner and
form as herein provided , sufficient to pay the cost
of constructing such building , and shall cause the
county court clerk to advertise for bids , and there-
after the fiscal court or board of commissioners , by
and through the county judge of such county , may
contract for the construction of such building .

CHAPTER 15 127
9. At or before the issuance of such bonds the
fiscal court or board of commissioners of such county
shall, by resolution , set aside and pledge the in-
come of such building into a separate and special
fund to be used and applied in payment of the cost
thereof and in the maintenance thereof . Said reso-
lution shall definitely fix and determine the amount
of revenue which shall be necessary and be set aside
and applied for the payment of the principal and
interest of the bonds , and balance of such income
shall be set aside for the reasonable and proper
maintenance thereof , including a sufficient sum to
pay the cost of such insurance as hereinabove pro-
vided . The rentals to be charged for the use of
such building shall be sufficient to provide for the
payment of interest upon all bonds and to create a
sinking fund to pay the principal thereof as and
when the same becomes due and to provide for the
maintenance thereof including the cost of insuring
such building erected against loss or damage by fire
and windstorm or other calamity.
§ 10. If any surplus shall be accumulated in the
maintenance fund , which shall be equal to the cost
of maintaining such building during the remaider
of the calendar , or fiscal year, as may be provided
by the resolution hereinbefore required and the cost
of maintaining and operating such building the suc-
ceedling like calendar or fiscal year, any excess over
such amount shall be transferred to the sinking fund .
§ 11. Such county may issue refunding bonds
for the purpose of providing for the payment of
any outstanding bonds , in accordance with the pro-
cedure prescribed by this act . Such refunding bonds
shall be secured to the same extent and shall have
the same source of payment as the bonds which shall
have been thereby refunded .

128 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 12. Should the fiscal court or board of com-
missioners find that the bonds authorized will be in-
sufficient to accomplish the purpose desired , addi
tional bonds may be authorized and issued subject
to the procedure as herein required .
§ 13. Such county in acquiring any building un-
der the provisions of this act , may provide by resolu-
tion any such provision and stipulation for the ad-
ministration of the income and for the security of
the bondholders as the fiscal court or board of com-
missioners of such county may deem necessary :
Provided , that the sinking fund , which shall be pro-
vided by resolution as hereinbefore required , shall
be deposited in a depository selected by such fiscal
court or board of commissioners , which deposit ,
where practicable , may be continuously secured by
a pledge to the county of direct obligations of the
United States of America , exclusive of accrued in-
terest , at all times at least equal to the balance on
deposit in such account , or in such other manner ac-
ceptable to the purchaser or holders of such bonds
Such securities shall either be deposited with the
county or be held by a trustee or agent satisfactory
to the fiscal court or board of commissioners of such
county. Such sinking fund may be invested in direct
obligations of the United States of America .
§ 14. When such board of education has paid
rentals , in the manner and form as provided in this
act , sufficient to amortize the cost of the erection of
such building and appurtenances , to maintain such
building and pay the cost of insurance , such county
shall thereupon convey said premises to such board ,
and shall transfer the balance , if any , remaining in
the funds herein provided to the account of such
board of education .
15. This act shall be deemed to create an addi-
tional and alternate method for the acquisition of

CHAPTER 16 129
elementary and/or high school buildings by the
county and shall not be deemed to include , alter ,
amend or repeal any other statute .
16. Whereas , many of the counties to which
this act is applicable are now without adequate ele-
mentary and/or high school buildings , thus en-
dangering the health , safety , convenience and gen-
eral welfare of the children thereof , now, therefore ,
an emergency is hereby declared to exist and this act
shall take effect immediately upon its passage and
approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 16 .
AN ACT to repeal , re -enact and amend Section 547 , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the liability of
stockholders of a corporation and the enforcement and limita-
tion thereof ; and providing further that any Life Insurance
Company heretofore or hereafter organized under the laws of
this Commonwealth , may issue non -assessable preferred capi-
tal stock of one or more classes , to which the double liability
provision shall not apply , and declaring an emergency exists .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 547 of Kentucky Statutes be ,
and it is hereby , amended by repealing said Section
in its entirety and re -enacting in lieu thereof the fol-
lowing :
" Section 547. Stockholders ; Liability of ; Action
to enforce ; Limitation .— The stockholders of each
corporation shall be liable to creditors for the full
amount of the unpaid part of stock subscribed for
by them , and no stockholder shall be liable because
of being a stockholder , for any sum more than to
the amount of the unpaid part of stock held by such
stockholder of any company , except stockholders in
Acts- 5

130 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
banks , trust companies , guaranty companies , invest-
ment companies and insurance companies shall be
liable equally and ratably , and not one for the other ,
for all contracts and liabilities of such corporation
to the extent of the amount of their stock at par
value , in addition to the amount of such stock , but
persons holding stock as fiduciaries shall not be per-
sonally liable as stockholders , but the estates in their
hands shall be liable , in the same manner and to the
same extent as the property of other stockholders ,
and no transfer of stock shall operate as a release
of any such liability existing at the time of such
transfer , provided the action to enforce such liability
shall be commenced within two years from the time
of transfer . And provided , further that any life in-
surance company heretofore or hereafter organized
under the laws of this Commonwealth , may issue non-
assessable Preferred Capital Stock of one or more
classes to which the double liability does not apply . "
Whereas , there now exists unusual economic con-
ditions , by reason of which it is in the public interest
that life insurance companies heretofore or here-
after organized under the laws of this Common-
wealth be permitted to issue and sell non -assessable
Preferred Stock in order to more adequatly provide
for the protection of their insured , and
Whereas , it is deemed by the General Assembly
to be both advisable and necessary that this author-
ity be forthwith granted to such insurance compa-
nies , an emergency is hereby declared to exist , and
this Act shall become effective upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved June 7 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 17 131
CHAPTER 17.
AN ACT authorizing , empowering , ordering and directing the
Auditor of Public Accounts and the Treasurer to refund to
all purchasers of motor vehicle licenses for the year 1934
any rates or amounts charged them in excess of the rates or
amounts fixed by subsection d of Section 2739g -2, Kentucky
Statutes , as re -enacted by the 1934 regular session of the
General Assembly , prescribing the manner of accomplishing
the purposes hereof and declaring an emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That , whereas , Subsection d of Section 2739g -2 of
Baldwin's Revision of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1932 Supplement , a part of Chapter 81 of the Acts
of the 1930 Session of the General Assembly , clas-
sifying certain motor vehicles as trucks and provid-
ing for registration fees of such motor vehicles , has
been repealed and re -enacted as emergency legisla-
tion by the 1934 Regular Session of the General As-
sembly , has been signed by the Governor , and is
now the statutory and existing law of this Common-
wealth .
And , whereas , the effect of the said repeal and re-
enactment of the aforesaid section of the Statutes
is a substantial reduction in the registration or
license fees for said motor vehicles for the year 1934 ,
and to fix the amounts chargeable for said licenses
for the year 1934 ,
And , whereas , by reason of an act of the Governor
of the Commonwealth extending the time for the
purchase or obtention of licenses for motor vehicles
for the year , 1934 , said license fees were not due
at the time the aforesaid legislation became effective .
And , whereas , the various county clerks of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , prior to the repeal and
re-enactment of Subsection d of Section 2739g -2 of
Kentucky Statutes , held themselves out as agents of

132 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Commonwealth for the sale and dispensation
of such motor vehicle licenses for the year 1934 ,
And , whereas , many citizens and residents of the
Commonwealth purchased licenses for the year 1934
for such motor vehicles at the rates existing before
said license fees for the year 1934 were reduced as
aforesaid , and before they were due and fixed as
aforesaid ;
That the Auditor of Public Accounts and the
Treasurer of the Commonwealth are authorized , em-
powered , ordered and directed to refund to all pur-
chasers of 1934 licenses , for all motor vehicles with-
in the purview of Subsection d of Section 2739g -2 as
repealed and re -enacted by the 1934 regular session
of the General Assembly, any excess amounts col-
lected from them for said licenses for the year 1934
over and above the rates and amounts fixed by Sub-
section d of Section 2739g -2 as re- enacted as afore-
said : Provided that no claims for refunds shall be
recognized when not accompanied with the license
certificate of the claimant or an attested copy there-
of , and no such claim shall be recognized which was
not filed and presented before July 15 , 1934. The
Auditor of Public Accounts shall determine the
manner of accomplishing the purposes hereof .
Whereas the action of the Governor of this Com-
monwealth in extending the time for the purchase
of 1934 automobile licenses has been ratified by the
1934 regular session of the General Assembly in
reducing the said licenses in compliance to the re-
quest of the Governor ,
And , whereas , the situation thus created has re-
sulted in a gross discrimination and injustice against
many citizens who did not avail themselves of the
extension of time granted by the Governor and
bought their licenses before the aforesaid reduction

CHAPTER 18 133
was effected and before the said license fees were
due ,
An emergency is declared to exist and this act
shall become a law and take effect immediately after
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved June 12 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 18 .
AN ACT relating to the licensing of motor vehicle operators and
providing for the definition of terms ; and that operators shall
be licensed ; that certain persons exempted from license ;
certain exemptions for non -residents from license ; what per-
sons shall not be licensed ; stipulating age limits for drivers
of school buses and public passenger motor vehicles ; instruc-
tion permits ; application for operators ' license and minors ;
certain waiver of examination ; designation of Circuit Court
clerks as examiners ; register of operators to be maintained ;
duplicate license certificates ; license to be signed and car-
ried ; expiration of license ; courts to report convictions and
may recommend suspension of license ; mandatory suspension
or revocation of license ; discretionary suspension or revoca-
tion of license ; right of appeal ; new license after revoca-
tion ; liability of parent , guardian or employer , or owner
for negligence of minor operator ; and , further providing for
the violation of license provisions ; making false affidavit
perjury ; making it unlawful to permit unlicensed minor to
operate motor vehicle ; or to permit violations of this act ; or
to operate motor vehicle while license suspended or revoked ;
and , further providing, for penalties for the violation of the
provisions of this act ; uniformity of interpretation ; short
title ; constitutionality ; and repeal ; providing for the distribu-
tion of the revenue derived from this Act , and declaring an
emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. Definition of Terms :
The following words and phrases when used in
this Act shall for the purpose of this Act have the
meanings respectively ascribed to them in this sec-

134 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion , except in those instances where the context
clearly indicates a different meaning .
(a) "Vehicle " . Every device in, upon or by
which any person or property is or may be trans-
ported or drawn upon a public highway , excepting
devices moved by human and animal power or used
exclusively upon stationary rails or trucks .
(b ) " Motor Vehicle " . Every vehicle , as herein
defined , which is self-propelled .
(c ) " Farm Tractor " . Every motor vehicle de-
signed and used primarily as a farm implement for
drawing plows , sowing machines and other imple-
ments of husbandry.
(d ) " Person " . Every natural person , firm , co-
partnership , association or corporation .
( e ) " Owner " . A person who holds the legal title
of a vehicle or in the event a vehicle is the subject
of an agreement for the conditional sale or lease
thereof with the right of purchase upon performance
of the conditions stated in the agreement and with
an immediate right of possession vested in the con-
ditional vendee or lessee , or in the event a mortgagor
of a vehicle is entitled to possession , then such con-
ditional vendee or lessee or mortgagor shall be
deemed the owner for the purpose of this Act .
(f) "Operator " . Every person, other than a
chauffeur , who is in actual physical control of a
motor vehicle upon a highway .
(g) " Chauffeur " . Every person who is employed
for the principal purpose of operating a motor ve-
hicle and every person who drives a motor vehicle
while in use as a public or common carrier of per-
sons or property .
(h( " Non -resident " . Every person who is not a
resident of this State .
(i ) " Highway " . Every way or place of what-
ever nature open as a matter of right to the use of

CHAPTER 18 135
the public for purposes of vehicular travel . The
term " highway " shall not be deemed to include a
roadway or driveway upon grounds owned by
private persons , colleges , universities , or other in-
stitutions .
(j ) " Department " . The State Tax Commission .
(k) " Commissioner " . The State Tax Commis-
sion Chairman .
§ 2. Operators Shall be Licensed .
Every person except those expressly exempted
under Sections 3, 4, 7 and 13 of this Act , before
operating any motor vehicle upon any highway in
this Commonwealth , shall procure a Driver's Li-
cense . The first license under this Act shall be ob-
tained not later than September 1 , 1934. License
shall cost fifty ( 50c ) cents , fifteen ( 15c ) cents to be
retained by the examiner to defray expenses for ad-
ministering the provisions of this Act, and ten ( 10c )
cents shall be forwarded to the Department by the
examiner for the use of the Department for printing
of necessary forms , inspections and expenses for
administering the provisions of this Act , and the ad-
ditional twenty-five ( 25c ) cents derived from the
provisions of this Act shall be forwarded to the
Auditor of Public Accounts into the State Treasury
and credited to a revolving fund maintained for the
purposes of paying salaries and expenses for the
enforcement of the motor vehicle laws of the Com-
monwealth , said fund to be expended in the manner
determined by the Governor of the Commonwealth ,
and warrants to be issued by the Auditor of Public
Accounts against said funds for the aforesaid pur-
poses upon the order of the Governor of the Com-
monwealth .
§ 3. Persons Exempt from License.
(a) No person shall be required to obtain an
operator's license for the purpose of driving or

136 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
operating a road roller , road machinery , or any farm
tractor or implement of husbandry temporarily
drawn , moved or propelled on the highways .
(b) Every person in the service of the Army ,
Navy or Marine Corps of the United States and
when furnished with a driver's permit and when
operating an official vehicle in such service shall be
exempt from license under this act .
§ 4. Non -Residents , When Exempt from License .
(a ) A non - resident over the age of sixteen years ,
who has been duly licensed either as an operator
under a law requiring the licensing of operators in
his home state or country and who has in his im-
mediate possession either a valid operator's license
issued to him in his home state or country shall be
permitted without examination or license under this
Act to drive a motor vehicle upon the highways of
this State .
( b ) It shall be unlawful for any non -resident
whose home state or country does not require the
licensing of operators and who has not been licensed
either as an operator in his home state or country ,
to operate any motor vehicle upon any highway in
this state without first making application for and
obtaining a license as an operator , as required for
and obtained a license as an operator as required
under this Act , except that any unlicensed non -resi-
dent who is over the age of sixteen years and who
is a resident of a state not requiring operators '
licenses may operate any motor vehicle which has
been duly registered for the current calendar year
in the state or country of which the owner is a resi-
dent upon the highways of this state for a period of
not more than thirty ( 30 ) days in any one year with-
out making application for or obtaining an opera-
tors ' license under this Act , upon condition that such
non -resident may be required at any time or place

CHAPTER 18 137
to prove lawful possession or the right to operate
such motor vehicle and establish his proper identity .
(c) Any non- resident or other persons whose
operator's license or right or privilege to operate a
motor vehicle in this state has been suspended or
revoked as provided in this Act shall not operate a
motor vehicle in this state under a license , permit or
registration certificate issued by any other jurisdic-
tion , or otherwise operate a motor vehicle in this
state during the period of such suspension , or with-
in one year after the date of such revocation . Any
person operating a motor vehicle in violation of this
section shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon
conviction shall be punished as provided in Section
29 of this Act .
5. What Persons Shall Not be Licensed :
(a ) An operator's license shall not be issued to
any person under the age of sixteen years .
(b) The Department shall not issue an opera-
tor's license to any person whose license as opera-
tor has been suspended during the period for which
such license was suspended , nor to any person whose
license as operator has been revoked under the pro-
visions of this Act until the expiration of one year
after such license was revoked .
( c ) The Department shall not issue an opera-
tor's license to any person who it has determined is
an habitual drunkard or is addicted to the use of
narcotic drugs.
(d ) No operator's license shall be issued to any
applicant who has previously been adjudged insane
or an idiot , imbecile , epileptic , or feeble -minded , and
who has not at the time of such application been re-
stored to competency by judicial decree or released
from a hospital for the insane or feeble -minded upon
a certificate of the superintendent that such person
is competent , nor then, unless the Department is

138 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
satisfied that such person is competent to operate a
motor vehicle with safety to persons and property .
( e ) The Department shall not issue an opera-
tor's license to any person when in the opinion of
the Department such person is afflicted with or suf-
fering from such physical or mental disability or
disease as will serve to prevent such person from
exercising reasonable and ordinary control over a
motor vehicle while operating the same upon the
highways , nor shall a license be issued to any per-
son who is unable to understand highway warning or
direction signs in the English language .
§ 6. Age Limits for Drivers of School Buses and
Public Passenger-Carrying Motor Vehicles :
It shall be unlawful for any person , whether
li-
censed under this Act
, or not
, who
isunder the age
ofeighteen years
,
todrive
amotor vehicle while
in
use of
aschool bus for the transportation of pupils
toor from school
orfor any person
, whether licensed
under this Act or not
, who
isunder the age of twen-
ty
-one years
todrive
amotor vehicle while in use as
apublic passenger
-carrying vehicle
.

7. Instruction Permits
:
The Department upon receiving from any person
over the age
ofsixteen years an application for
a
temporary instruction permit may
inits discretion
issue such
apermit entitling the applicant
, while
having such permit
inhis immediate possession
,
to
drive

aperiod
ofsixty

60) days when accompanied by

operator
,who

inthe vehicle
.

8. Application for Operator's License
:
(a) Every application for an operator's license
shall be made upon the approved form furnished
by the Department and shall be verified by the ap-

CHAPTER 18 139
plicant before a person authorized to administer
oath .
(b) Every application shall state the name , age ,
sex and residence address of the applicant , and
whether or not the applicant has heretofore been
licensed as an operator and if so , when and by what
state , and whether or not such license has ever been
suspended or revoked and if so , date and reason for
such suspension or revocation .
§ 9. Application of Minors :$
The Department shall not grant the application
of any minor under the age of eighteen years for an
operator's license unless such application is signed
by the father of the applicant , if the father is living ,
and has custody of the applicant , otherwise by the
mother or guardian having the custody of such
minor , or in the event a minor under the age of
eighteen years has no father , mother or guardian ,
then an operator's license shall not be granted to
the minor unless his application therefor is signed
by his employer .
10. Waiver of Examination :
The Department may in its discretion issue an
operator's license under this Act without examina-
tion to every person applying therefor within three
months after this section takes effect and who is of
sufficient age , as required by Section 5 of this Act ,
to receive the license applied for and who furnishes
evidence to the Department that such applicant has
previously operated any motor vehicle in a satis-
factory manner within this State over a period of
not less than one year .
11. Designation of Circuit Court Clerk :
The Circuit Court Clerk of each county is hereby
authorized and designated to act for the Department
for the purpose of issuing operators ' licenses , and
keeping in his office at all times , available to the De-

140 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
partment , a record of all licenses issued in his coun-
ty, and all violations occurring within his county
under this Act. It shall be the duty of the Clerk to
designate or appoint some person , or persons , to
conduct examination of applications for operators '
licenses under the provisions of this Act. It shall be
the duty of the Clerk to make a written report of
his findings and recommendations upon such exam-
ination to the Department .
§ 12. Register of Operators :
The Department shall file every application for
an operator's license and index the same by name
and number and maintain suitable records of all
licenses issued and all applications for licenses de-
nied, also a record of all licenses which have been
suspended or revoked .
13. Licenses Issued to Operators :
(a ) The Department shall issue to every person
licensed as an operator an operator's license . Any
person licensed as a chauffeur shall not be required
to procure an operator's license , but no person shall
drive any motor vehicle as a chauffeur unless il-
censed as a chauffeur .
(b) Every such license shall bear thereon the
distinguishing number assigned to the licensee and
shall contain the name , age , residence address and
a brief description of the licensee for the purpose
of identification, also a space for the signature of
the licensee .
( c ) The Department upon determining after an
examination that an applicant is mentally and phy
sically qualified to receive a license , may issue to
such person a temporary driver's permit entitling
such person while having such permit in his im-
mediate possession to drive a motor vehicle upon the
highways for a period of ten days before issuance
to such person of an operator's license ,

CHAPTER 18 141
§ 14. Duplicate License Certificates :
In the event that an operator's license issued un-
der the provisions of this Act shall be lost or de-
stroyed , the person to whom the same was issued
may obtain a duplicate or substitute thereof upon
furnishing proof satisfactory to the Department that
such license has been lost or destroyed and upon
payment of twenty-five cents ( 25c ) .
§ 15. License to be Signed and Carried :
( a ) Every person licensed as an operator shall
write his usual signature with pen and ink in the
space provided for that purpose on the license cer-
tificate issued to him immediately upon receipt of
such certificate , and such license shall not be valid
until the certificate is so signed .
( b ) The licensee shall have such license in his
immediate possession at all times when driving a
motor vehicle and shall display the same upon de-
mand of a Justice of the Peace , a peace officer or a
field deputy or inspector of the Department . It shall
be a defense to any charge under this subsection
that the person so charged produce in court an
operator's license , theretofore issued to such per-
son and valid at the time of his arrest .
§ 16. Expiration of Licenses :
Every operator's license issued hereunder shall be
valid until suspended or revoked , as provided in
this Act .
17. Court to Report Convictions and May
Recommend Suspension of License :
Every court having jurisdiction over offenses com-
mitted under this Act , or any other Act of this state
regulating the operation of motor vehicles on high-
ways , shall , through the Circuit Clerk of the County
in which the offense was committed , forward to the
Department a record of any conviction of any per
son in said court for a violation of any said laws ,

142 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and may recommend the suspension of the operator's
license of the person so convicted , and the Depart-
ment shall thereupon consider and act upon such
recommendation in such manner as may seem to it
best .
§ 18. Mandatory Suspension or Revocation of
license by the Department :
(a ) The Department shall forthwith revoke the
license of any person upon receiving a record of the
conviction of such person of any of the following
crimes :
1. Manslaughter resulting from the operation of
a motor vehicle .
2. Driving a vehicle while under the influence of
intoxicating liquor , or narcotic drug .
3. Perjury or the making of a false affidavit to
the Department under this Act or any other law of
this State requiring the registration of motor ve-
hicles or regulating their operation on highways .
4. Any crime punishable as a felony under the
motor vehicle laws of this State of any other felony
in the commission of which a motor vehicle is used .
5. Conviction or forfeiture of bail upon three
charges of reckless driving all within the preceding
twelve months .
6. A conviction of a driver of a motor vehicle ,
involved in an accident resulting in the death or
injury of another person upon a charge of failing
to stop and disclose his identity at the scene of the
accident .
(b) The Department upon receiving a record of
the conviction of any person upon a charge of operat-
ing a motor vehicle while the license of such person
is suspended or revoked , shall immediately extend
the period of such first suspension or revocation for
an additional like period .

CHAPTER 18 143
$ 19. Department May Suspend orSuspend or Revoke
Licenses :
(a ) The Department may immediately suspend
the license of any person without hearing and with-
out receiving a record of conviction of such person
of crime whenever the Department has reason to be-
lieve :
1. That such person has committed any offenses
for the conviction of which mandatory revocation of
license is provided in Section 18 .
2. That such person has , by reckless or unlawful
operation of a motor vehicle , caused or contributed
to an accident resulting in death or injury or serious
property damage .
3. That such person is incompetent to drive a
motor vehicle or is afflicted with mental or physical
infirmities or disabilities rendering it unsafe for such
person to drive upon the highways .
4. That such person is an habitual reckless or
negligent driver of a motor vehicle or has committed
a serious violation of the motor vehicle laws of this
state .
Whenever the department suspends the license of
any person for any reason set forth in the above
four paragraphs , the department shall immediately
notify the licensee and afford him an opportunity of
a hearing before said department in the county
wherein the licensee resides , and upon such hearing
the department shall either rescind its order of sus-
pension , or good cause appearing therefor , may sus-
pend the license of such person for a further period
or revoke said license .
(b) The department is hereby authorized to sus-
pend or revoke the right of any non -resident to op-
erate a motor vehicle in this state for any cause for
which the license of a resident operator may be sus-

144 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pended or revoked , and any non -resident who op-
erates a motor vehicle upon a highway when his
right to operate has been suspended or revoked by
the department shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
subject to punishment as provided in Section 30 .
( c ) The department is hereby authorized to sus-
pend or revoke the license of any resident of this
state upon receiving notice of the conviction of such
person in another state of an offense therein which
if committed in this state , would be grounds for the
suspension or revocation of the license of an oper-
ator . The department is further authorized upon
receiving a record of the conviction in this state of
a non -resident driver of a motor vehicle of any of-
fense under the motor vehicle laws of this state , to
forward a certified copy of such record to the motor
vehicle administrator in the state wherein the per-
son so convicted is a resident .
( d ) The department shall not suspend a license
for a period of more than one year and upon sus-
pending or revoking any license shall require that
such license so suspended or revoked shall be sur-
rendered to and retained by the department except
that at the end of a period of suspension such
license so surrendered shall be returned to the
licensee .
$ 20. Right of Appeal :
The hearing granted the licensee under
Section 19 of this Act shall be held under the same
legal procedure and rights of parties as in criminal
trials in the county court.
Any person denied a license , or whose license has
been suspended and/or revoked by the department
shall be granted an appeal from the order of the De-
partment denying such license , or from the order
or judgment of the department on hearing , to the

CHAPTER 18 145
county court of the county , wherein the licensee re-
sides , which appeal and trial in the county court
shall be governed by the same procedure as on ap- ·
peals from inferior courts to the county court , and
the chief agent or representative of the department
in said county shall be the agent of the department
for the service of all summons of other court process
on all hearings , appeals or trials under the provis-
ions of this act . The licensee shall be granted a
speedy trial in the county court after due notice by
summons , but by agreement of parties the trial may
be held at any time after filing of appeal .
The licensee may further pray and shall be
granted an appeal from any final order or judgment
entered or rendered against him in said
county court, to the circuit court of said county and
the appeal and trial shall be the same as other cases
in the circuit court on appeal , except the case may
be heard before the circuit judge during vacation by
agreement of parties .
The county and circuit courts are hereby vested
with jurisdiction under the provisions of this act .
The licensee shall be entitled to a change of venue
in the circuit court as provided by law .
§ 21. New License after Revocation :
Any person whose license is revoked under this
Act shall not be entitled to apply or receive any new
license until the expiration of one year from the date
such former license was revoked .
§ 22. When Parent , Guardian , or Employer Lia-
ble for Negligence of Minor :
Any negligence of a minor under the age of
eighteen years licensed upon application signed as
provided in Section 9 , when driving any motor ve-
hicle upon a highway , shall be imputed to the person
who shall have signed the application of such minor

146 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for said license , which person shall be jointly and
severally liable , with such minor , for any damages
by such negligence .
§ 23. Owner Liable for Negligence of Minor :
Every owner of motor vehicle causing or know-
ingly permitting a minor under the age of eighteen
years to drive such vehicle upon a highway , and any
person who gives or furnishes a motor vehicle to
such minor , shall be jointly and severally liable with
such minor for any damages caused by the negli-
gence of such minor in driving such vehicle .
§ 24. Violation of License Provisions :
It shall be unlawful for any person to commit any
of the following acts :
(a) To display or cause to permit to be dis-
played or to have in possession any operator's
li-
cense knowing the same to be fictitious or

CHAPTER 18 147
$ 25. Making False Affidavit Perjury :
Any person who shall make any false affidavit , or
shall knowingly swear or affirm falsely , to any mat-
ter or thing required by the terms of this Act to be
sworn to or affirmed , shall be guilty of perjury and
upon conviction shall be punished by fine or impris-
onment as other persons committing perjury are
punishable .
$ 26. Unlawful to Permit Unlicensed Minor to
Drive Motor Vehicle :
orIt shall be unlawful for any person to cause
knowingly permit any minor under the age of
eighteen years to drive a motor vehicle upon a high-
way as an operator , unless such minor shall have
first obtained a license to so drive a motor vehicle
under the provisions of this Act .
$ 27. Unlawful to Permit Violations of Act :
No person shall authorize or knowingly permit a
motor vehicle owned by him or under his control to
be driven by any person who has no legal right to do
so or in violation of any of the provisions of this
Act.
§ 28. Unlawful to Drive While License Sus-
pended or Revoked :
Any person whose operator's license has been sus-
pended or revoked , as provided in this Act , and who
shall drive any motor vehicle upon the highways of
this state while such license is suspended or revoked ,
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon convic-
tion shall be punished as provided in Section 29 of
this Act .
$ 29. Penalty for Misdemeanor :
(a) It shall be a misdemeanor for any person to
violate any of the provisions of this Act unless such
violation is by this Act or other law of this state de-
clared to be a felony .

148 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(b ) Unless another penalty is in this Act or by
the laws of this state provided , every person con-
victed of a misdemeanor for the violation of any pro-
vision of this Act shall be punished by a fine of not
less than five dollars ($ 5.00 ) nor more than one
hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) or by imprisonment for
not more than six months , or by both such fine and
imprisonment .
§ 30. Penalty for Driving While License Sus-
pended or Revoked :
Any person convicted of a violation of Section 28
of this Act shall be punished by imprisonment in the
county jail for a period of not less than two days nor
more than six months and there may be imposed in
addition thereto a fine of not less than five dollars
($5.00 ) nor more than one hundred dollars
( $ 100.00 ) .
§ 31. Uniformity of Interpretation :
This act shall be so interpreted and construed as
to effectuate its general purpose to make uniform
the laws of this commonwealth .
§ 32. Short Title :
This act may be cited as the " Uniform Operator's
License Act ."
§ 33. Constitutionality :
If any part or parts of this Act shall be held to be
unconstitutional such unconstitutionality shall not
affect the validity of the remaining parts of this Act .
§ 34. Whereas the eighteenth amendment of the
Constitution of the United States has been repealed
and the enactment of the Alcoholic Control Act in
Kentucky by the regular session of the General As-
sembly of 1934 and there being great need for imme-
diate control of the drivers of motor vehicles and
whereas the finances of the State Treasury are de-

CHAPTER 19 149
pleted , therefore , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this bill shall become a law and effective
immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
JAS . H. THOMPSON ,
Presiding officer of the Senate .
Approved July 3 , 1934 .
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .
CHAPTER 19 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , repealing , amending
and re -enacting Chapter 101 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of 1934 , defining passenger automobiles , providing for
registration fees of such motor vehicles and declaring an
emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Chapter 101 of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of 1934 , regular session , be repealed ,
amended and re -enacted so that when said section is
re-enacted it shall read as follows :
Automobiles primarily designed for carrying pas-
sengers and having provisions for not more than
seven passengers , including the operator , shall be
classed as passenger automobiles and the annual
registration fees for same shall be as follows :
For each passenger car having less than 22 horse-
power , Six Dollars ( $ 6.00 ) ; for each such car hav-
ing 22 horse -power and less than 32 horse -power ,
Seven Dollars and twenty cents ( $ 7.20 ) ; for each
such car having 32 horse -power or more , Twelve
Dollars ( $ 12.00 ) , and " horse -power " within the
meaning of this act shall be calculated on the rating
fixed by the Society of Automobile Engineers .

150 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
2. The registration fee for each motorcycle
shall be Three Dollars ( $ 3.00 ) , and for each side-
car attached thereto , One Dollar and Fifty Cents
( $1.50 ) .
§ 3. In addition to the license tax imposed by
Sections 1 and 2 above , there is hereby imposed on
each motor vehicle , subject to license in this State ,
a tax equal to three (3% ) per centum of the regular
retail sales price of said motor vehicle , said retail
sales price to be determined by the price which said
vehicle sells in the county in which license is applied
for, by regularly authorized dealers in said motor
vehicle , if a new car, and if a used car by the price
of value given in the Automotive Reference Manual
used by the State Tax Commission . Provided , how-
ever , that said additional tax shall not apply or be
collected if said motor vehicle was purchased by or
came into the hands of the person seeking the license
prior to July 1 , 1934 , or was purchased under such
circumstances that the retail price of said motor
vehicle to the purchaser was increased by the addi-
tion or imposition of the Kentucky Gross Receipts
Tax of 1934. And , provide , further , that said
additional tax imposed by this section shall only be
collected once .
§ 4. The Kentucky State Tax Commission is
hereby authorized to promulgate rules and regula-
tions for the purpose of making this Act effective ,
and prepare and furnish to the various county
clerks of this Commonwealth forms and blanks nec-
essary for said purpose .
Whereas, the Kentucky Gross Receipts Tax law of
1934 is now in effect and ,
Whereas, it is desirable that Kentucky dealers in
automobiles be placed on a parity with their com-
petitors in other states , an emergency is declared to

CHAPTER 20 151
exist and this Act shall become a law and be effective
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 20 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , repealing, amending
and re -enacting Chapter 102 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of 1934 , defining motor vehicles classed as trucks ;
providing for registration fees of such motor vehicles , and
declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Chapter 102 , of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of 1934 , regular session , be repealed ,
amended and re -enacted so that when said section is
re-enacted it shall read as follows :
That all motor vehicles , excluding such as are
mentioned in sub - section c of Section 2739g -2 , Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 edition , and exclud-
ing motor vehicles engaged in hauling passengers
for hire , are classified as trucks and the annual reg-
istration fee shall be as follows :
Those having a capacity of one thousand pounds
or less , ten ( $10.00 ) dollars ; those having a capacity
of more than one thousand pounds and up to two
thousand pounds , twenty-two ( $ 22.00 ) dollars ; those
having a capacity of more than two thousand pounds
and up to three thousand pounds , forty ( $40.00 ) dol-
lars ; those having a capacity of more than three
thousand pounds and up to four thousand pounds ,
fifty -two ( $52.00 ) dollars ; those having a capacity of
more than four thousand pounds and up to five
thousand pounds , sixty -three ( $ 63.00 ) dollars ; those
having a capacity of more than five thousand pounds
and up to six thousand pounds , seventy -one ( $ 71.00 )

152 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
dollars ; those having a capacity of more than six
thousand pounds and up to seven thousand pounds ,
ninety-three ( $ 93.00 ) dollars ; those having a capac-
ity of more than seven thousand pounds and up to
eight thousand pounds , one hundred and twelve
($112.00 ) dollars ; those having a capacity of more
than eight thousand pounds and up to nine thousand
pounds , one hundred and thirty -eightand thirty -eight ( $138.00 ) ;
those having a capacity of more than nine thousand
pounds and up to ten thousand pounds , one hundred
and eighty- seven ( $ 187.00 ) dollars ; those having a
capacity of more than ten thousand pounds , two
hundred and thirty ( $230.00 ) dollars ; and twenty-
five ( $ 25.00 ) dollars for each additional ton or frac-
tion of a ton in excess of ten thousand pounds . The
applicant for license for a motor truck shall state
in his application the capacity of the truck .
§ 2. The registration fees as provided herein ,
the issuance of receipts duplicates , tags , and the
transfer of such receipts and tags , and the account-
ing for fees , and all other things required to be done
in connection with the same , shall be done as , and at
the time and in the manner as is now or may here-
after be provided by law . One -half of all moneys
raised by this tax shall be evenly distributed among
the one hundred and twenty counties , one two hun-
dred and fortieth ( 1/240 ) of all truck licenses going
to each of the counties , for the county road funds .
§ 3. In addition to the license tax imposed by
Section 1 above , there is hereby imposed on each
motor vehicle , classed as truck , subject to license in
this state , a tax equal to three ( 3 % ) per centum of
the regular retail sales price of said motor vehicle ,
said retail sales price to be determined by the price
which said vehicle sells in the county in which license
is applied for , by regularly authorized dealers in

CHAPTER 21 153
said motor vehicles , of a new car , and if a used car
by the price of value given in the Automotive Refer-
ence Manual used by the State Tax Commission .
Provided , however , that said additional tax shall not
apply or be collected if said motor vehicle was pur-
chased by or came into the hands of the person seek-
ing the license prior to July 1 , 1934 , or was pur-
chased under such circumstances that the retail
price of said motor vehicle to the purchaser was in-
creased by the addition or imposition of the Ken-
tucky Gross Receipts Tax of 1934. And , provided
further , that said additional tax imposed by this sec-
tion shall only be collected once .
§ 4. The Kentucky State Tax Commission is
hereby authorized to promulgate rules and regula-
tions for the purpose of making this Act effective ,
and prepare and furnish to the various county clerks
of this Commonwealth forms and blanks necessary
for said purpose.
Whereas , the Kentucky Gross Receipts Tax law of
1934 is now in effect , and ,
Whereas , it is desirable that Kentucky dealers in
automobiles be placed on a parity with their com-
petitors in other states , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall become a law and be effective
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 21 .
AN ACT to regulate the Department of Mines and Minerals of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; to provide for the appoint-
ment and employment of officers and employees thereof ; to
designate the chief officer of said department as the Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals ; to prescribe the
qualifications of such officers and employees and define their
duties , rights and privileges , and to fix their salaries and pro-

154 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
vide for the payment of their expenses ; to provide offices for
said Department , and necessary and proper equipment , in-
struments , appliances , apparatus , trucks , chemicals , stationery ,
postage , and other things , for the use of said department and
its officers and employees ; to provide for the supervision and
inspection of all commercial coal mines by said Department
of Mines and Minerals of this Commonwealth , and to pro-
vide for the safety and protection of persons employed in and
about mines and of mining property and other property used
in connection with mines ; to prescribe the duties of mine
owners and operators and their employees respecting the
planning , equipment , management and operation of mines and
the methods of work and labor therein and in connection
therewith ; to provide that said Department and its officers
and employees shall operate and be protected by the provi-
sions of the Workmen's Compensation Laws of Kentucky ; to
provide penalties for violations of this act and to repeal
certain statutes and all laws in conflict herewith levying a
fee on all coal operators to be collected by the Department of
Mines and paid into the State Treasury and credited to the
General Expenditure Fund ; and declaring an emergency to
exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Definitions .
Mine : In this Act the term " Mine " includes the
shafts , slopes , drifts , or inclined planes connected
with excavations penetrating coal stratum or strata ,
which excavations are ventilated by one general air
current , or divisions thereof , and connected by one
general system of mine railroads over which coal
may be delivered to one or more points outside the
mine .
Excavations and Workings : The term " Excava-
tions " and workings includes all the excavated por-
tions of the mine , those abandoned as well as the
places actually being worked ; also all underground
workings and shafts , tunnels and other ways in the
course of being sunk or driven , slopes , tunnels and
other openings and all such shafts , together with

CHAPTER 21 155
all roads , appliances , machinery and material con-
nected with the same below the surface .
Shaft : The term " Shaft " means a vertical open-
ing through the strata that is or may be used for the
purpose of ventilation or drainage , or for hoisting
men , coal or material , or either , in connection with
the mining of coal .
Slope : The term " Slope" means an inclined
opening used for the same purpose as a shaft .
Drift : The term "Drift " means an opening
through the strata , on which opening grades are
such as to permit the coal to be hauled by mules or
mechanical traction power, and which opening may
be used for the purpose of ventilation , drainage , in-
gress , egress and other purposes in connection
with the mining of coal .
Operator : The term " Operator " means any
firm , corporation , or individual operating any coal
mine , or any part thereof.
Superintendent : The term " Superintendent"
means the person who shall have on behalf of the
operator , immediate supervision of one or more
mines .
Mine Foreman : The term " Mine Foreman "
means the person whom the operator or superin-
tendent shall place in charge of the workings of the
mine and of persons employed therein. He is re-
quired to be certificated and of seeing that the pro-
visions of the Mining Law are complied with .
Assistant Mine Foreman : The term " Assistant
Mine Foreman " means the person designated to as-
sist the mine foreman in the immediate supervision
of a portion or the whole of a mine and of the per-
sons employed therein.
Fire Boss : The term " Fire Boss " means any
person whom the operator or superintendent is re-

156 ACTS OF THE GENERAL Assembly
quired to employ under certain conditions with ref-
erence to explosive gases in this Act designated ,
when same are found to exist in a mine . He is re-
quired to be certificated and is charged with the
duties of preserving the safety of the persons em-
ployed in such mines with reference to ventilation
and gases .
Shot-Firers : The term " Shot-Firers " mean a
practical and experienced person whose duty shall
be to charge , set off and discharge the shots in all
blastings in the workings of said mine when deemed
advisable by the operator or superintendent to do
so ; or when ordered to do so in gaseous mines by the
district inspector and approved by the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals . Said shot - firers
shall at all times be under the direction of the mine
foreman .
District Mine Inspector : The term " District
Mine Inspector " means a person recommended by
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
and commissioned by the Governor of the State . He
is required to be certificated and is charged with the
inspection of mines and to see that the requirements
of this Act are complied with .
Inspector of Mine Weights : The term " Inspec-
tor of Mine Weights " means a person having the
same qualifications as a district mine inspector . He
is required to have a general knowledge of mine
scales and be thoroughly competent to inspect and
test the same .
Check Weighman : The term " Check Weigh-
man " means the person elected by a majority of the
miners engaged in digging or mining coal at a mine
to act as their representative and see that all coal
mined or loaded by said miners is properly weighed
and accounted for .

CHAPTER 21 157
Approved Safety Lamps : The term " Approved
Safety Lamp " shall mean any bonneted safety lamp
which bears the approval plate of the Federal
Bureau of Mines and is approved by the State De-
partment of Mines and Minerals .
Gaseous Mine : A " Gaseous Mine " is one in
which the percentage of explosive gas exceeds one-
half of one per cent at the return of any one split in
a dusty mine and exceeds one and one -fourth per
cent in the return of any one split in a non -dusty
mine .
Commercial Mine : The term " Commercial Mine "
means any coal mine from which coal is mined for
sale or exchange
Tipple or Dumping Point : The term " Tipple or
Dumping Point " means the tipple or structure
where coal is dumped or unloaded from the mine car
into railroad cars , trucks , wagons or other means of
conveyance .
The provisions of this Act shall apply to all com-
mercial coal mines , and where six or more persons
are employed in a period of twenty-four hours , the
operator of any such mine shall be required to em-
ploy a certified mine foreman therein to properly
carry out the plans and rules when approved by the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals and
to see that the Mining Law is complied with as re-
quired in this Act .
§ 2. Duties of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals . The supervision of all laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky relating to mines is vested in
the Department of Mines and Minerals .
§ 3. Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals , His Designation , Appointment , Term of Office ,
Salary and Expenses .-The chief officer of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals shall be designated

158 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
as the " Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals ." The salary of the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals shall be $4,200.00 dollars per
annum , payable monthly as other salaries are paid
and he shall likewise be allowed and paid his neces-
sary traveling and other expenses incurred on ac-
count of and when engaged in the discharge of his
official duties . Immediately after the effective date
if the Act , the Governor of Kentucky shall appoint
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
who shall serve for a term of four years , subject to
removal by the Governor at his pleasure .
§ 4. Qualifications of the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals .—The Chief of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals shall be a male citi-
zen of Kentucky and shall be a competent person ,
having had at least fifteen ( 15 ) years mining experi-
ence , ten ( 10 ) of which having been in and about
the mines in this state and he shall be thoroughly
familiar with all methods of safety pertaining to the
operation of mines , he shall have a practical knowl-
edge of the different systems of working and ven-
tilating coal mines and of the nature , chemistry , and
properties of noxious and poisonous gases , also of
explosive gases , also of the dangers due to such
gases and the prevention thereof and of the dangers
incident to blasting and the prevention thereof ; of
the methods for the prevention of explosives in
mines due to gas or dust ; of the methods for the
management and extinguishing of mine fires ; of the
methods for rescue and relief work in mine disas-
ters ; of the application of electricity in mining oper-
ations ; of the application of mechanical loading in
mining operation , also of equipment and explosives
manufactured for use in coal mines ; of the mechan-
ical features of mine scales and the methods used in

CHAPTER 21 159
properly inspecting and testing same ; of the
methods used in locating oil and gas wells when
drilled through any of the coal seams within the
state ; also the proper manner of drilling and plug-
ging of said oil and gas wells ; he shall have a prac-
tical knowledge of mining engineering and be able to
efficiently report on any proposed development in
mining operations or the possibilities of operating
any of the coal seams within the state .
§ 5. Oath and Bond .- Said Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals shall before he enters
upon the discharge of his duties , be sworn to dis-
charge said duties faithfully and impartially , which
oath shall be in writing and certified by the officer
administering it, and said oath and certificate shall
be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and
said Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall give bond in the penal sum of two thousand
dollars , with surety , to be approved by the Governor
for the faithful discharge of his duties . Said Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals shall be
liable to dismissal by the Governor for willful neg-
lect of duty, or misconduct or malfeasance in office .
§ 6. Offices.--The main offices of the Department
of Mines and Minerals shall be located in the Mining
Engineering Building of the State University at
Lexington , where adequate offices shall be furnished
it on account of the mutual problems the Depart-
ment may have with the Mining Department of the
University , also the facilities of the Mining Labora-
tory . The Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals shall have the necessary engineering , sten-
ographic and clerical assistance to properly and ef-
ficiently carry out the duties of his office . The offices
of the respective district inspectors shall be at such
places within their districts as the Chief of the De-

160 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
partment of Mines and Minerals may designate and
they shall be allowed office rent not to exceed twenty
dollars ( $20.00 ) a month in the discretion of the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals .
§ 7. Duties of the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals .-Said Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals shall have full authority over
said department and he shall superintend and direct
the activities of the various inspectors and other
personnel of the department . He shall have author-
ity to divide the coal fields of the state from time to
time into such number of inspection districts as may
be necessary to accord with the number of inspec-
tors in such manner as to equalize as nearly as prac-
ticable the work of each inspector and to assign to
the inspectors their appropriate districts . When-
ever the Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals may deem it necessary in the interest of ef-
ficent supervision of the mines to temporarily em-
ploy the services of two or more inspectors or when-
ever he may deem it desirable in the interest of ef-
ficient inspection to change inspectors from one dis-
trict to another he shall have the authority to do so .
He shall superintend and direct the inspection of
mines as herein provided and he shall cause to be in-
vestigated the character and quality of air in mines
whenever conditions indicate the necessity of so do-
ing ; he shall collect statistics relating to coal mining
within the state each year and make report of same ;
see that maps , plans , projections and proposed de-
velopments of all coal mines are made and filed in
his office , subject to his approval , according to the
provisions of this act ; keep a record of all inspec-
tions made by himself and the different members of
the department , which shall be a permanent record
properly indexed and he shall perform all other

CHAPTER 21 161
duties elsewhere in this Act assigned to him . He
shall also have general supervision of the training
of officials and workmen in safety and first aid and
mine rescue methods and the conducting of demon-
strations in safety when deemed advisable by him to
do so ; also the dissemination of information among
officials and employees concerning mine ventilation ,
mining methods and mine accidents and their pre-
vention , assume full charge in the event of mine fire
or explosion or other serious accident at any mine
within the state . He may, in his discretion , assist
in the resumption of operations of any mine or
gather data for the development of any coal seams
that would be of any benefit to the state or create
employment for persons not already employed .
$ 8. Stationery , Postage , Etc.- The Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals and the various
inspectors shall be provided with all necessary sta-
tionery and postage for the conduct of official cor-
respondence as other state officers are supplied . The
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall be supplied with all printed blank forms ,
notices and other printed matter necessary for use
in the performance of his duties , upon his requisi-
tion therefor through the superintendent of public
printing .
§ 9. There shall be provided for the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals and all inspec-
tors such instruments, appliances , apparatus , chemi-
cals , and vehicles as are necessary for the proper
discharge of his official duties , which shall be paid
for on the order of the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals and which shall belong to the
State . The cost of repairs to such instruments , ap-
paratus and vehicles as may be necessary from time
to time shall be paid for by the inspector having
Acts- 6

162 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
such repairs made , and be included in his account
of expenses incurred on account of official duties and
paid as they are paid .
§ 10. Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals Shall Furnish Certified Copy of Inspection
Report .- The Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals shall furnish to any person a certified copy
of the report of inspection of any mine inspected by
himself or by a District Inspector , when ordered to
do so by any circuit court judge , which copy shall be
admissible in evidence in any court in this Common-
wealth , and shall be prima facie evidence of the
truth of the recitals therein contained .
§ 11. Annual Report of the Department of Mines
and Minerals .-The Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals shall annually make report to
the Governor of the Commonwealth his proceedings
for and during the calendar year ending on the
thirty -first day of December . Said Annual Report
shall be prepared and printed within four months
and as much earlier as possible after the close of the
calendar year . Said Annual Report shall list the
complete operation of the coal mines in this state ,
enumerating all accidents which have occurred in
or about the same , and giving such other informa-
tion as said Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals may deem useful , and making such sug-
gestions as he may deem important as to further
legislation on the subject of mining .
§ 12. Operator's Monthly Report .- For the
purpose of enabling the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals to keep records and make re-
ports as is required by this act , the owner , lessee or
superintendent of every mine to which this law ap-
plies is hereby required to give , each month , accu-
rate information on blanks to be furnished by the
{

CHAPTER 21 163
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals as
to all accidents that have occurred ; number of per-
sons employed ; amount of coal mined ; and such
other information not of a private nature as the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals may
request .
§ 13. Operator's Annual Report .- The owner ,
lessee , or superintendent of each mine to which this
law applies shall also be required to give at the end
of each calendar year ending December thirty-first
accurate information on blanks to be furnished by
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals ,
as to the number of accidents that have occurred ,
the number of persons employed , the tons of coal
mined at such mine , and such other information not
of a private nature as the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals may request .
§ 14. Mine Rescue Divisions .-The Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals is hereby author-
ized and directed to partition the coal fields of the
state into divisions which shall be known as mine
rescue divisions of which there shall be not less than
two in the western coal field and not less than four
in the eastern coal field . In each division there shall
be a station, the location of which shall be deter-
