mined by the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals , at which station shall be kept apparatus ,
appliances and supplies for use in the work of rescue
and relief upon the occurrence of explosions or mine
fires , and for the training of officials and miners in
such work .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Miner-
als is hereby directed to provide each station ,
as it shall be located , and himself with such instru-
ments , appliances , apparatus, chemicals , and trucks

164 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
as may be necessary and he shall designate one of
his district inspectors to have charge of each station .
The supplies and repairs necessary for the instru-
ments , appliances , apparatus , chemicals , and trucks
and other equipment shall be purchased by the Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals as needed
from time to time and paid for as other expenses are
paid , on the order of said Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals .
§ 15. Governor to Appoint District Inspectors
and Inspectors of Mine Weights —also Their Qualifi-
cations . The Governor shall appoint not exceeding
ten District Mine Inspectors and not exceeding four
Inspectors of Mine Weights . All said District Mine
Inspectors and Inspectors of Mine Weights shall
have a thorough knowledge of first aid and mine
rescue and shall be able to instruct in first aid and
mine rescue and shall have a thorough knowledge of
the different systems of ventilating , timbering and
working coal mines and of the nature , chemistry and
properties of noxious and poisonous gases , also of
explosive gases . Also the danger due to such gases
and the prevention thereof , the dangers incident to
blasting and the prevention thereof, also methods
for the prevention of explosions in mines due to gas
or dust ; also methods for the management or ex-
tinguishing of mine fires and methods for rescue
and relief work in mine disasters ; also the applica-
tion of electricity in mining operations ; also of the
mechanical features of mine scales and the proper
method of inspecting and testing same ; and shall
have a thorough and practical knowledge of mining
gained by at least ten ( 10 ) years experience at and in
coal mines , at least five ( 5 ) years of which must have
been in and about the mines in this state . Said Dis-
trict Inspectors and Inspectors of Mine Weights

CHAPTER 21 165
shall not , while holding office , act in any official ca-
pacity in operating any coal mine and he shall be
of good moral character and temperate habits . Said
District Mine Inspectors and Inspectors of Mine
Weights shall serve for a term of four years , sub-
ject to removal by the Governor at his pleasure , and
whose salaries shall not be less than $ 2,400.00 dol-
lars nor more than $2,700.00 dollars per annum , pay-
able monthly and shall likewise be allowed and paid
their necessary traveling and other expenses in-
curred on account of and when engaged in the dis-
charge of their official duties . Said salaries to be de-
termined by the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals and approved by the Governor and
based on ability and experience of the Inspector .
Said District Inspectors and Inspectors of Mine
Weights before entering upon the discharge of their
duties shall be sworn to discharge those duties faith-
fully and impartially and to the best of their skill
and ability , which oath shall be certified by the of-
ficer administering it and said oath , in writing , and
certificate shall be filed in the office of the Secretary
of State , each of said District Inspectors and Inspec-
tors of Mine Weights shall give bond in the penal
sum of one thousand dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) with surety
to be approved by the Governor for the faithful dis-
charge of his official duties .
$ 16. Certification of District Mine Inspectors .
-Before any person shall be appointed to the office
of District Mine Inspector , he shall be required to
pass a satisfactory examination before the Board of
Examiners hereinafter in this Act provided for . The
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals is
hereby authorized to recommend to the Governor of
the State applicants for District Mine Inspector who
have successfully passed such examination and who

166 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
are proved by worth , training and experience to be
the most competent of such applicants .
§ 17. Duties of District Mine Inspectors .— Each
of said District Inspectors shall give his entire time
and attention to the duties of his office which shall
consist of the inspection of coal mines , and of aiding
under the direction of the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals , in carrying out and enforc-
ing the provisions of the laws relating to the inspec-
tion of such mines ; also of training officials and
workmen in and about the mines in First Aid and
Mine Rescue methods ; also of advising officials and
workmen in methods pertaining to safety in all its
phases ; also in methods pertaining to mine fires and
explosions ; also of taking charge of mine rescue and
recovery work when mine fires , mine explosions , and
other serious accidents occur within their districts ,
in the event the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals should not be present ; also to assist in
such work in other districts when so directed by the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals ;
also to reopen mines or portions of mines that have
been sealed on account of fire or any other cause ,
when called upon by the owner or operator and when
directed by the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals so to do .
§ 18. Reports of the District Inspectors .-Each
district Inspector shall report in writing weekly to
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
the number and conditions of all mines inspected by
him each week , and he shall deliver to the operator
or superintendent of each mine inspected a state-
ment in writing showing the inspection of such
mine , and the condition thereof as found upon such
inspection . He shall also , within fifteen days after
the close of each calendar year , file with the Chief

CHAPTER 21 167
of the Department of Mines and Minerals a report
of his proceedings during said calendar year and
give such information concerning the mines and
mining conditions in his district as may be appro-
priate and required by the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals . He shall visit each mine in
his district at least once every three months , or
oftener if practicable to do so , and make a personal
examination of the interior of each mine with re-
spect to ventilation , drainage , timbering , blast-
ing , electricity and general security as well as the
outside of the mine , where any danger to the work-
men may exist , and he shall see that all provisions of
this Act are strictly carried out .
§ 19. Ventilation of Mines .- Should the district
inspector discover any room , entry, airway or other
working places being driven in advance of the air
currents contrary to the requirements of this Act , he
shall notify the mine foreman to cease work in such
places at once until the law is complied with .
The ventilation of all coal mines at which as many
as five men are employed shall be produced by means
of fans mechanically operated , located outside the
mine . Mines at which less than five men are em-
ployed shall be ventilated by means approved by the
Department of Mines and Minerals . Provided , how-
ever , that when inside fans are required to ventilate
working places on longwall or other modern systems
of mining , the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Mineral must first approve such installation .
In case of accident to the ventilating fan , or its
machinery whereby the ventilation of the mine
would be seriously interrupted , the mine foreman
shall order the men to withdraw immediately from
the mine , and he shall not allow them to return to
their work until the ventilation has been restored

168 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and the mine has been thoroughly examined by him ,
or by an assistant mine foreman or fire boss , and the
mine is reported safe .
Any operator , or superintendent of a coal mine
before making any new or additional openings , from
the outside , shall submit to the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals , for his information and
approval , a map or plan showing the proposed sys-
tem of mining the coal seam and the proposed sys-
tem of ventilation and equipment of the openings
with their locations and relative positions to adja-
cent developments . In order that the records of the
Department of Mines and Minerals be complete as
to all operating mines the operator or superintend-
ent of a coal mine who has not previous to the pas-
sage of this Act submitted to the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals a map or plan showing
the proposed system of mining and the proposed
system of ventilation and equipment of the openings
with their locations and relative positions to adja-
cent developments , shall for his information and ap-
proval submit a map or plan forthwith ; and no such
new or additional openings shall be made until ap-
proved by the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals .
The operator, superintendent or mine foreman
of every coal mine , whether worked by shaft , slope
or drift , shall provide and hereafter maintain for
every such mine adequate ventilation affording no
less than one hundred cubic feet of air per minute
for each and every person employed in the portion
of the mine affected thereby and two hundred cubic
feet of air for each animal working in the part of
such mine affected thereby , which shall be circulated
around the main headings and cross headings and
room headings and working places to an extent that

CHAPTER 21 169
will dilute , render harmless and carry off any nox-
ious and dangerous gases therein . As working
places advance , break throughs for air shall be made
sixty feet apart , except where companies now are
permitted to drive with the approval of the District
Inspector and the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals to ninety feet before turning break
throughs . Provided , however , where longwall or
modern systems of mining are employed the Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals shall have
the authority to approve , at his discretion , a greater
distance between break throughs or the method of
properly ventilating such longwall or modern sys-
tems of mining . All the break throughs between the
intake and return airways not required for the pass-
age of air shall be closed with stoppings substanti-
ally built with suitable material , which shall be ap-
proved by the district inspector , so as to keep work-
ing places well ventilated .
As many as sixty persons shall be permitted to
work in the same air current , or split , provided ,
however , that with the approval of the district in-
spector and the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals . As many as eighty persons may be
permitted to work in the same air current , or split .
No operator , superintendent or mine foreman
shall permit any persons to work where they are
unable to maintain a sufficient amount of ventilation
but this shall not be construed to prohibit the
operator from employing men to make the places
of employment safe to comply with this require-
ment ; provided , further , that while the repair work
necessary to get the mine in condition to comply
with the law is in progress , no person or persons
shall be permitted to enter that part of the mine
affected except those actually employed in doing the

170 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
necessary repair work , said repair work to be done
under the constant supervision of an official desig-
nated by the mine foreman.
No product of petroleum , or alcohol , or any com-
pound that in the opinion of the inspector will con-
taminate the air to such an extent as to be injurious
to the health of the miner shall be used as motive
power in any mine .
In all mines liberating explosive gas the minimum
ventilation shall be one hundred and fifty cubic feet
of air per minute , for each and every person em-
ployed therein and three hundred cubic feet of air
per minute for each animal worked therein and as
much more as the district inspector may deem req-
uisite, and all stoppings shall be substantially built
of suitable material subject to the approval of the
district inspector so as to keep the working places
well ventilated ; doors on main haulways shall be
avoided in gaseous mines where practicable , and
over casts built of concrete , or other suitable mate-
rial and of ample strength , shall be adopted , and
where doors are used they must be built in a sub-
stantial manner and hung so as to close automati-
cally when unobstructed .
All unusued workings and abandoned parts of the
mines must be protected by such safeguards as will
prevent, so far as practicable the accumulation or
overflow of gas therein , and all avenues leading
thereto shall be so arranged and conducted as to
give cautionary notice to all persons of the danger
in entering therein ; and in order to secure safety
of the workmen in general against the danger
in said unused or abandoned section of the mine ,
notice shall be posted warning all persons not to
enter such parts of said mine , except persons author-
ized to make examination of such sections and it

CHAPTER 21 171
shall be unlawful for any person, except as afore-
said , to enter such parts of said mine .
$ 20. Accumulation of Coal Dust .-In all mines
accumulations of fine dry coal dust shall , as far
as practicable , be removed from the mine and all dry
and dusty operating sections kept thoroughly
watered or rock dusted or dust allayed by such other
methods as may be approved by the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals .
$ 21. Shafts , Slopes , and Drifts .-It shall not be
lawful for the operator , superintendent , or mine
foreman of any coal mine , whether worked by shaft ,
slope or drift , to permit persons to work therein un-
less there are to every seam of coal worked in
such mine at least two separate outlets , separated
by natural strata of not less than one hundred feet in
breadth if the mine be worked by shaft or slope , and
separated by natural strata of not less than fifty feet
in breadth if the mine be worked by drift- by which
outlets distinct means of ingress and egress are
readily available to persons employed in such mine ;
but it shall not be necessary for the two outlets to
belong to the same mine .
The foregoing requirements shall not apply to the
openings of a new entry of a mine that is being
worked for the purpose of making connection
between said two outlets so long as not more than
four persons are employed at one time in making the
connection or driving the second opening ; nor shall
said requirements apply to any mine in which the
second opening has been rendered unavailable by
reason of final robbing or removal of pillars so long
as not more than twenty persons are employed
therein at one time .
In case any coal mine has but one shaft , slope , or
drift for ingress and egress of the persons employed

172 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
therein, and the owner thereof does not own suitable
ground for another opening , such owner may select
appropriate associate adjacent ground for that pur-
pose and have the same condemned and appropriate
the same by proceedings in the county court of the
county wherein the mine is situated similar to pro-
ceedings now allowed for securing a private pass-
way .
In every shaft used as an escape outlet there shall
be provided a stairway or ladderway of suitable
strength , design , and angle , which shall be provided
with platforms or landings at each turn of the stair-
way or ladderway, the whole to be subject to the ap-
proval of the district inspector and the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals , or in lieu of such
stairway or ladderway there shall be provided a
properly equipped hoisting arrangement , consisting
of a cage suitable for hoisting men , a wire hoisting
rope of ample strength , and an adequate and safe
hoisting engine and drum, which hoisting arrange-
ment shall be ready for use at any time of emer-
gency . When the escape and fan shafts are com-
bined , the side or end used for an escape outlet and
the part used for ventilating purposes shall be sepa-
rated by a good , substantial air -tight partition from
top to bottom , so that the use of the escape shaft
shall not in any way interfere with the ventilation
of the mine . All shafts by which men enter or leave
the mine and the passageways to escape outlets shall
be carefully examined by the mine foreman , or by a
person designated by him , at least once each week
that the mine may be operating, and the data and
findings of such examination shall be entered in a
book kept at the mine for that purpose. No debris ,
such as falls of slate and similar material , which
shall act as an obstruction to the free passage of

CHAPTER 21 173
men , shall be allowed to accumulate in the passage-
way to the escape outlet , nor shall bodies of water
difficult to pass be allowed to accumulate there , nor
shall the passageway be obstructed by mine cars or
timbers . If obstructions of any sort to the free pas-
sage of men be found , such obstruction shall be
promptly removed . At points where the passage-
way to the escape outlet is intersected by roadways
or entries calculated to produce doubt as to the
proper direction to the outlet , conspicuous sign-
boards shall be placed indicating the direction it is
necessary to take to reach the exit for escape .
At every mine operated by shaft, there shall be
provided an approved safety catch , and a sufficient
and substantial cover overhead on all cages used for
lowering and hoisting persons , and at the top of each
shaft a safety gate shall be provided . An adequate
brake , so adjusted that it may be operated by the
engineer without leaving his post at the levers , shall
be attached to every drum or equivalent machine
used for the lowering or raising persons at all shafts
or slopes , and an efficient indicator or dial shall be
used in connection with the hoisting engine which
will show the engineer the positions of the cages in
the shaft at any time ; and approved means for sig-
naling to the bottom man , the top man , and engineer
shall be provided and all shafts used for lowering
or hoisting men shall be equipped with metal tubes ,
pipes , or telephones , through which conversations
may be freely held between persons at the bottom
and top landings No cages on which men are riding
shall be lifted or lowered at a race of speed greater
than six hundred feet per minute . The hoisting
rope , the safety catches and the cage attachments
shall be examined by the mine foreman or some com-
petent person designated by him, every morning be-
fore the men descend into the mine ; and care shall

174 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be taken to keep them in good working condition at
all times . All persons are prohibited from riding
on cages when coal or slate or similar material is
being raised , and no person shall enter a cage at the
bottom to be raised to the top during the running
hours of the mine , or when leaving work at the close
of the day's run , without first being authorized by
the bottom -man or cager to do so , said bottom -man
or cager having first signaled to the engineer that
men are to be raised . At every underground land-
ing where persons enter or leave the cage and where
persons must pass from one side of the shaft to the
other there shall be a passageway not less than
three feet wide and of suitable height , kept free
from obstruction , and as dry as possible , around the
shaft ; and all employees when passing from one side
of the shaft to the other side shall use such passage-
way only . Whenever the hoisting or lowering of
men occurs before daylight or after dark , and when
the landing at which men take or leave the cage is
obscured by steam or otherwise , there shall be main-
` tained at each landing a light sufficient to show the
landing and objects in immediate proximity there-
to , and as long as men are underground a good light
shall be maintained at the bottom of the shaft , so
that persons coming to the bottom may discern the
cage and objects in the vicinity thereof .
§ 22. Checking Employees in and Out of Mine.—
The operator or superintendent of every mine shall
install a uniform system of checking the employees
in and out of the mine , whereby each employee upon
entering the mine shall be given a check by which
he shall be identified , and which check shall be
placed in its proper place on the check board by the
employee when leaving the mine .
$ 23. Shaft , Slope or Incline . No operator or
superintendent of any coal mine worked by shaft ,

CHAPTER 21 175
slope or incline shall place in charge of any engine or
drum used for lowering or hoisting any persons em-
ployed in such mine any but competent and sober
engineers or drum runners ; and no engineer in
charge of such machinery shall allow any person
except such as may be designated for this purpose
by the operator or superintendent to interfere with
any part of the machinery ; and no person shall in-
terfere with or intimidate the engineer or drum run-
ner in the discharge of his duties and in no case
where the mine is operated or worked by shaft ,
slope or incline shall more than ten persons ride in
any cage or car at one time , without the approval of
the district inspector and the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals first having been ob-
tained , and no person shall ride on a loaded cage or
car in any shaft or slope or on any incline . Provided
however , where special man cars or cages are em-
ployed to haul workmen on inclines the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals shall have the
authority to regulate the method and procedure of
said handling of man cars or workmen .
§ 24. First Aid Equipment .- In every mine
where fifty or a less number of men are employed
underground, it shall be the duty of the operator
thereof to keep always on hand at the mine one
properly constructed stretcher , one woolen and
waterproof blanket , and all necessary requisites
which may be advised by the Department of Mines
and Minerals and for each additional fifty men so
employed , one additional stretcher and equipment
as above specified shall be furnished .
§ 25. Scope of Inspection , Etc.- The operator
or superintendent of every coal mine shall furnish
the inspector proper facilities for entering such
mine and making examinations or obtaining in-
formation ; and if any inspector shall discover that

176 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
any mine does not in respect to appliances for the
safety of the persons employed therein conform to
the provisions of this Act , or that by reason of any
defect or practice in or at or about such mine the
lives or health of persons employed therein , are en-
dangered , he shall immediately orally and in writ-
ing, notify such operator or superintendent thereof,
stating in such notice the particulars in which he
considers such mine to be defective or dangerous
and if he deems it necessary for the protection of
the lives and health of the persons employed in such
mine he shall, after giving notice to the said opera-
tor or superintendent , in writing , notify immedi-
ately the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals shall forthwith summons the operator, superin-
tendent , and the district inspector to discuss at the
mine the unsafe practices , defects , or unhealthful
conditions in the mine and if the Chief of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals determines that for
the safety of the persons employed in said mine the
mine should be closed , he shall forthwith order the
mine to be closed until it is placed in a safe and
proper condition for mining operations ; the owner
or operator of any mine so closed may apply to the
circuit court wherein such mine is located , or the
judge thereof in vacation , by petition for an order
directing said mine to be reopened , and such court ,
or the judge thereof in vacation , shall immediately
hear and determine the matters arising upon such
petition , and if upon full hearing thereof the court ,
or the judge thereof in vacation shall find that said
mine is in a reasonably safe condition the prayer of
said petition shall be granted ; but notice of said
hearing shall be given to the district mine inspector
and the Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-

CHAPTER 21 177
erals three days at the least before said hearings
and in all such hearings the Attorney General, the
Commonwealth's Attorney , or the County Attorney
of the district or county in which the mine is situ-
ated , shall appear for the state and defend the same .
26. Approved Closed Lights , When to be Used
-In any mine or sections of mines liberating ex-
plosive gas where the lives of the persons employed
therein may be endangered , by the liberating of said
explosive gas the district inspector with the ap-
proval of the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals may direct the operator , or superintendent
of any mine or mines to work said mine or mines ex-
clusively by the use of approved safety lamps and
approved electric lamps . The operator or superin-
tendent of said mine or mines shall forthwith carry
out the recommendations of the district mine in-
spector when so approved by the Chief of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals .
In mines where flame safety lamps and approved
electric lamps are required to be used for working
therein, the lamps shall be in charge of some person
to be designated by the operator or superintendent ;
and at least two additional flame safety lamps shall
be kept at every coal mine whether such mine lib-
erates explosive gas or not. If deemed advisable by
the district inspector and the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals . All mine foremen and
fire bosses employed in gaseous mines shall , at all
times , carry an approved flame safety lamp for the
purpose of detecting the presence of explosive gas ,
said lamp to be kept lighted at all times when in use
inside of the mines .
In mines where approved safety lamps and ap-
proved electric lamps are used , no miner workman ,
or other person shall at any time enter any mine or
carry therein any intoxicating liquors , matches ,

178 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pipes , cigars , cigarettes , or any device for making
lights or fire not authorized or approved ; and in all
such mines the operator shall , at frequent intervals ,
search or cause to be searched , any person entering
or about to enter any mine , to prevent such persons
from taking or carrying therein any of the above
mentioned articles , and no person shall be permitted
to enter any mine while under the influence of intox-
icating liquors .
§ 27. In Event Explosion , Fire or Accident Oc-
curs . Whenever by reason of any fire , explosion or
other accident in any coal mine , or the machinery
connected therewith , loss of life or serious personal
injury shall occur , it shall be the duty of the super-
intendent of the mine , and in his absence , the mine
foreman in charge of the mine to give notice forth-
with to the inspector of the district stating the par-
ticulars of such accident ; and if any one is killed
thereby , to the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals ; and the said inspector shall immedi-
ately go to the scene of such accident and make such
investigation and such suggestions and render such
assistance as he may deem necessary for the future
safety of the men and investigate the cause , of such
fire, explosion or accident and make a record thereof
which he shall forward to the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals , the same to be pre-
served with the other records of his office ; and to
enable him to make such investigations , the Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals or the dis-
trict inspector shall have the power to compel the
attendance of witnesses , and to administer oaths
and affirmation .
§ 28. Abandoned Mines .—No person or persons
shall attempt to enter , explore or survey an aban-
doned mine or portion thereof without the approval
of the inspector of the district in which the mine is

CHAPTER 21 179
located or the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals .
Stable.-No$ 29. Under - Ground operator ,
superintendent or mine foreman shall provide a
horse or mule stable inside of any mine , unless it be
approved in writing by the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals .
§ 30. Steam Locomotive .- No steam locomotive
shall be used in any mine in this state unless it be
approved in writing by the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals .
$ 31. Explosive Magazines , Where to be Located .
-All distributing explosive magazines used for
quantities of explosives estimated as a daily supply
shall be located not less than one hundred feet from
any mine opening or building used or occupied by
any person or persons ; and the outside construction
of such magazines shall be of non -combustible ma-
terial .
All magazines used for storing powder or other
explosives in greater quantities than an estimated
daily supply shall be located not less than five hun-
dred feet from any mine opening or building used
or occupied by any person of persons ; and the out-
side construction of such magazines shall be of non-
combustible material , provided however , this shall
not apply to distributing or storage magazines al-
ready constructed prior to the passage of this act .
Where Cardox or other explosives of a similar
nature are manufactured at or near the mine , the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall have the authority to prescribe the location
and procedure of such installation .
§ 32. First Aid Training .—It shall be the duty
of each and every coal operator in this state to see
that all employees receive the necessary first aid
training for the safety of themselves and fellow-

180 AOTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
workmen as recommended by the State Department
of Mines and Minerals . It shall be the duty of each
employee to receive the necessary training .
§ 33. Electricity in Mines .- On all haulage
roads , landings and partings , where men are re-
quired to regularly work or pass under trolley or
other bare power wires which are placed less than
six and one-half feet above top of rail , a suitable pro-
tection shall be provided . This protection shall con-
sist of channeling the roof , placing boards along the
wires , which shall extend below it , or the use of other
approved devices that afford protection .
All machine feed wires shall be placed on glass or
porcelain insulators which shall be so placed as to
prevent the wires coming in contact with the coal .
When machine or feed wires are carried in same
entry as trolley wire , they shall be placed on the
same side as the trolley wire , between trolley wire
and rib and protected from contact therewith , and
positive feed wires crossing places where persons
or animals are required to travel , shall be safety
guarded or protected from such persons or animals
coming in contact therewith , as provided in this
section .
All trolley and positive feed wires shall be placed
on opposite sides of track from refuge holes or
necks of room , when so ordered by the Department
of Mines and Minerals , and wires may be placed
across the necks of rooms when protected as pro-
vided for in this section . Switches or circuit -break-
ers shall be provided to control the current at the
mine , and all important sections in the mine .
All power wires cables in hoisting shafts or man-
way compartments shall be properly insulated , sub-
stantially fixed and well protected .
§ 34. Locomotives in Gaseous Mines .—In gase-
ous mines the Chief of the Department of Mines and

CHAPTER 21 181
Minerals may designate where flameproof electric
locomotives shall be used .
$ 35. Electric Coal Cutting Machines .-In gase-
ous mines the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals may designate where flameproof electric
coal -cutting machines shall be used .
No man shall be placed in charge of a coal -cutting
machine in any gaseous mine or gaseous portion of
a mine who is not a competent person , capable of
determining the safety of the roof and the sides of
the working places and detecting the presence of ex-
plosive gas . Machine runners shall be compelled to
undergo an examination to determine their fitness
to detect explosive gas before they are permitted to
have charge of machines in mines liberating gas ,
unless they be accompanied by a certified fire boss ,
or a machine runner or helper having passed such
an examination . Said examination to be given by
the mine foreman and approved by the District In-
spector , blank forms for same to be furnished by the
Department of Mines and Minerals , a copy to be re-
tained on file at the mine office and the original to
be sent to the State Department of Mines and Min-
erals fully made out and signed by the machine run-
ner and the mine foreman and approved by the
District Inspector .
In any gaseous portion of a mine , a coal -cutting
machine shall not be brought within the last break
through next the working face , until the machine
man shall have made an inspection for gas in the
place where the machine is to work , unless such ex-
amination is then made by some other competent
person authorized and appointed for that purpose
by the mine foreman . If any explosive gas is found
in the place the machine shall not be taken in until
the gas is removed .

182 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
In working places where gas is likely to be en-
countered , a safety lamp , or other suitable approved
apparatus for the detection of fire damp , shall be
provided for use with each machine when working ,
and should any indication of fire damp appear on
the flame of the safety lamp, or other apparatus
used for detection of fire damp , the person in charge
shall immediately stop the machine , cut off the cur-
rent at the nearest switch , and report the matter to
the mine foreman , or fire boss , and the machine shall
not again be started in such place until the mine
foreman , fire boss , or a person duly authorized by
either has examined it and pronounced it safe . All
coal -cutting machines in mines where fire damp is
detected , shall be provided with a box specially de-
signed for carrying safety lamps for the protection
of such lamps .
No coal -cutting machine shall be operated in a
gaseous portion of a mine for a longer period than
ten minutes without an examination as above de-
scribed being made for gas , and if gas is found , the
current shall at once be switched off the machine ,
and the trailing cable shall forthwith be discon-
nected from the power supply .
$ 36. Fire Boss and His Duties .- In all gaseous
mines as defined in this Act the operator or superin-
tendent shall employ a fire boss or fire bosses , whose
competency to act as such shall be evidenced by a
certificate of qualification from the Board of Exam-
iners as provided in this Act . It shall be the duty
of the fire boss to examine carefully , before each
shift enters the mine , every working place without
exception , all places adjacent to live workings , every
roadway, every unfenced road to abandoned work-
ings , and falls in the mine ; but before proceeding
with the examination he shall see that the air cur-
rent is traveling its proper course . In making the

CHAPTER 21 183
examination he shall use no light other than that
inclosed in an approved safety lamp . The fire boss
shall examine for all dangers in all portions of the
mine under his charge , and after examination he
shall leave at the face of every place examined the
date of the examination , as evidence that he has
performed his duty. He shall also examine the en-
trance or entrances to all worked -out and abandoned
portions adjacent to the roadways and working
places under his charge where explosive gas is likely
to accumulate , and he shall place a danger signal
across the entrance to every working place and
every other place where explosive gas is discovered
or where immediate danger is found to exist from
any other cause , and said signal shall be sufficient
warning for persons not to enter .
A suitable record book shall be kept at the mine
office of every mine wherein fire bosses are employed ,
and immedately after the examination of such mine
or any portion thereof , by a fire boss , whose duty it
is to make such examination , he shall enter in said
book with ink or indelible pencil , a record of such
examination , and sign same . This record shall show
the time taken in making the examination , and also
clearly state the nature and location of any danger
that may have been discovered in any room or entry
or other place in the mine . If any danger or dangers
has or have been discovered , the fire bosses shall im-
mediately report the location thereof to the mine
foreman, or in his absence to the assistant mine fore-
man in charge , who in turn shall report to the mine
foreman . No person shall enter the mine until the
fire bosses return to the mine office , or to a station lo-
cated in the intake entry of the mine (where a
record book as provided for in this Section shall be
kept and signed by the person making the examina-
tion ) and report to the mine foreman or the assist-

184 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ant mine foreman , by telephone or otherwise , that
the mine is in safe condition for the men to enter .
When a station is located in any mine it shall be the
duty of the fire bosses to sign also the report entered
in the record book in the mine office on the surface .
The record books of the fire bosses shall at all times
during working hours be accessible to the District
Inspector .
The mine foreman and the fire boss shall at or
near the main entrance to the mine , provide a per-
manent station with a proper danger signal , desig-
nated by suitable letters and colors placed thereon .
In every mine in which fire bosses are required to be
employed by this Act, when the working portions
are one mile or more from the entrance to the mine
or from the bottom of the shaft or slope , a station
of suitable dimensions may be erected by the mine
foreman (provided the location is approved by the
District Inspector ) for the use of the fire bosses . It
shall not be lawful for any person , except the mine
foreman , and in case of necessity such other persons
as may be designated by him, to pass beyond said
station and danger signal until the mine has been
examined by a fire boss , as provided in this Section ,
and the mine or certain portions thereof reported by
him to be safe . The fire boss shall not allow any
other person or persons to enter or remain in any
portion of the mine through which a dangerous ac-
cumulation of gas is being passed into the ventilat-
ing current from any portion of the mine . He shall
report any violation of this section to the mine fore-
man at once .
Any employee or other person , except those here-
in above provided for , who passes any danger signal
into the mine , or into any portion of the mine , or
removes such danger signal before the mine has
been examined and reported to be safe , or any em-

CHAPTER 21 185
ployee or any other person who passes any danger
signal placed at the entrance to a working place , or
any other place in the mine , or removes such danger
signal without permission from the mine foreman ,
the assistant mine foreman , or the fire boss shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and it shall be the
duty of the mine foreman having knowledge of such
violation (whether obtained personally or other-
wise ) to notify the operator or superintendent at
once of such violation , who shall in turn notify the
District Inspector , in writing , and the District In-
spector shall forthwith institute proceedings against
such person or persons as provided for in this Act .
Any fire boss who neglects to comply fully with
the provisions of this Act relating to his duties , or
who shall make a false report of the condition of
any place in the portion of the mine allotted to him
for examination , shall be deemed guilty of a misde-
meanor , and shall be suspended by the mine fore-
man , and his name shall be given to the District In-
spector for prosecution . If he be found guilty , he
shall return his certificate of qualification as fire
boss to the Department of Mines and Minerals .
Nothing in this Article shall prevent a mine fore-
man holding a certificate of the first class from act-
ing as fire boss , or a regularly employed fire boss
from acting in an emergency as a mine foreman
holding such first class certificate .
$ 37. Short [ Shot ] Firers .-In any coal mine in
this state wherein the operator may choose to em-
ploy practical and experienced persons as shot firers
to prepare the cartridge and set off and discharge
the shots he may do so.
Provided , however , in gaseous mines the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals may require the opera-
tor to employ practical and experienced persons as
shot firers and the Department of Mines and Min-

186 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
erals may prescribe the conditions under which
blasting may be carried on and the kind of explo-
sives that shall be used .
§ 38. Solid Shooting.-In any mine in which
solid shooting is done the District Inspector is
authorized to prescribe the condition under which
solid shooting may be done .
$ 39. Some Duties of Operator or Superintend-
ent-Mine Supplies .- It shall be the duty of every
superintendent , on behalf and at the expense of the
operator, to keep on hand at or within convenient
distance of each mine at all times a sufficient quan-
tity of all materials and supplies required to pre-
serve the safety of the employees , as ordered by the
mine foreman and required by this Act . If for any
reason the superintendent cannot procure the neces
sary materials or supplies as aforesaid , he shall at
once notify the mine foreman, whose duty it shall
be to withdraw the men from the mine , or the por-
tion thereof operated , until such material or sup-
plies are received . The superintendent shall , at
least once every week , read and examine carefully ,
and countersign all reports entered in the mine
record book of the mine foreman .
The operator or superintendent shall co -operate
with the mine foreman and other officials in the
fulfillment of their duties are required by this Act ,
and he shall direct that the mine foreman and all
other employees under him comply with the law in
all its provisions , especially when his attention is
called by the District Inspector or the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals to any violations
of the laws .
The superintendent shall keep on hand at the
mine a supply of the printed rules and notices and
record books required by this Act , which record
books shall be furnished through the District In-

CHAPTER 21 187
spector , or through the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals , on request of the operator or
superintendent in writing . He shall see that said
rules and notices and record books are delivered to
the proper persons at the mine , and that they are
properly cared for, and he shall also see that the
rules and notices are posted in conspicuous places at
or near the entrance to the mine and kept in such
condition that they will always be legible .
The operator or superintendent of every mine
shall at least ten days preceding send to the Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals notices of
the following occurrences :
First-When a mine is about to be abandoned .
Second - When the working of a mine is to be
resumed after an abandonment or a discontinuance
for a period exceeding two months . Said mine shall
be inspected and meet the approval of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals before operations begin .
Third-When any change occurs in the name of a
mine , or in the name of the operator of a mine ,
under the provisions of this Act .
$ 40. Mine Foreman and His Duties .—In order
to better secure the proper ventilation of every coal
mine , and promote the health and safety of persons
employed therein the operator or superintendent
shall employ a competent and practical insire over-
seer , to be called mine foreman , who shall be a male
citizen and resident of this state , having had at least
five years practical experience in the working ,
ventilation and drainage of coal mines and who shall
hold a certificate of competency for such position is-
sued to him by the Department of Mines and Miner-
als, after taking an examination held by the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals under its rules and reg-
ulations for such examinations . When the mine
workings become so extensive that the mine foreman

188 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
is unable personally to carry out the requirements of
this Act pertaining to his duties , the operator or su-
perintendent shall employ a sufficient number of per-
sons having had at least three years experience in
coal mines to act as assistants to the mine foreman ,
who shall act under his instructions in carrying out
the provisions of this Act . The mine foreman shall
be responsible for their conduct in the discharge of
their duties under such employment .
man .
The duties of the mine foreman shall be to exer-
cise general supervision over the ventilating appa-
ratus and the airways , traveling ways and working
places , pumps and drainage , and shall see that as the
miners advance their excavations proper break
throughs are made as required by law to properly
ventilate the mine . He shall also see that the work-
men are provided with sufficient props , cap pieces ,
and timbers of suitable size , which shall be delivered
to the working place when requisitioned by the work-
He shall also see that the props are cut or
sawn as square as practicable at both ends and as
near as practicable to the proper length required or
designated for the places where they are to be used .
The mine foreman shall see that the water is drained
so near as practicable out of the working places , and
that the working places are kept as free from water
as practicable during working hours ; and the owner
lessee , or operator of all mines to which this Act
applies shall see that the water so drained or
pumped from mines shall be drained as directly as
practicable to the adjacent streams or watercourses
by means of ditches , flumes , pipes , sewers or other
adequate provisions .
It shall be the duty of the mine
that the break throughs are made ,
law ; that the ventilation shall be
foreman to see
as required by
conducted by

CHAPTER 21 189
means of said break throughs through the rooms by
means of check doors or curtains placed on the en-
tries or other suitable places , and he shall
not permit any room to be opened in advance of the
ventilating current . The mine foreman shall meas-
ure the air current with an anemometer to be fur-
nished by the operator at least once each month at
the inlet and outlet and at or near the faces of the
advanced headings , and shall keep a record of such
measurements in a book furnished and having a
form prescribed by the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals . Sign boards directing the way
to outlets or escape way shall be conspicuously
placed throughout the mine .
On single -track haulage roads in mines , where
haulage is done by machines , which roads the per-
sons employed in the mine must use while perform-
ing their work or while traveling on foot to and from
their work , there shall be places of refuge on one
side not less than three feet in depth from the side
of the mine car, and four feet wide , and not more
than sixty feet apart . On all haulage roads on which
haulage is done by draft animals , whereon men are
obliged to be in the performance of their duties or
when passing on foot to and from their work , there
shall be places of refuge not less than thirty inches
in depth from the side of the mine car , and four feet
wide , and not more than sixty feet apart . Provided ,
however , that special refuge places shall not be re-
quired in haulways on which room necks or break
throughs occur at regular intervals not exceeding
sixty feet , and thus furnish places of refuge , or on
haulways in which the track is so laid as to give a
minimum clearance on one side of not less than
thirty inches from the top of any haulage engine or
any mine car , said clearance to be on the side of the

190 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
haulway opposite that upon which electric wires are
strung, in event any such wires be strung in the haul-
ways . No person shall travel on foot to or from his
work upon any slope used for haulage or upon any
haulage road when other roads in proper condition
for travel are available .
On all main haulways , where hauling is done by
machinery of any kind , the mine foreman shall pro-
vide a proper system of signals , and a conspicuous
light on the front and a light or marker on the rear
of every trip or train of cars when in motion in the
mine .
It shall be the duty of the operator , superintend-
ent , or mine foreman to have bore -holes kept not less
than twelve feet in advance of the face , and , where
necessary , on the sides , if the working places that
are being driven are in dangerous proximity to an
abandoned mine , or part of a mine suspected of con-
taining inflammable gases , or which are filled with
water . If there be a map of such abandoned mine , or
part of a mine , said bore -holes shall be started when
the working places shall have approached within
fifty feet of such abandoned mine , or part of mine ,
but if there be no such map , then said bore- holes
shall be started when the advanced working places
are within one hundred feet of where said aban-
doned workings are supposed to be.
It shall be the duty of the mine foreman , or the
assistant mine foreman of every coal mine in this
state , to see that every person employed to work in
such mine shall before beginning to work therein , be
instructed in the particular danger incident to his
work in such mine , and furnished a copy of the rules
of such mine .
The mine foreman or his assistants shall visit and
carefully examine each working place in the mine

CHAPTER 21 191
each day or oftener while the miners of such places
are at work, and shall direct said miners so that each
and every working place shall be secured by props
or timbers where necessary . Should the mine fore-
man or his assistant find a place to be in a dangerous
condition, they shall not leave the place until it is
made safe , or remove the persons working therein
until the place is made safe by some competent per-
sons designated for that purpose .
The mine foreman shall see that every mine liber-
ating explosive gas is kept free of standing gas in
all working places and roadways . Any accumula-
tions of explosive gas or noxious gases in the worked
out or abandoned portions of any mine shall be re-
moved as soon as possible after its discovery. All
places in live sections that are temporarily aban-
doned shall be examined as live workings by the fire
boss on regular inspections . No person who may be
endangered by the presence of said explosive gas or
noxious gases shall be allowed in that portion of the
mine until said gases have been removed . The
mine foreman shall direct and see that all dangerous
places and the entrance of entrances to worked out
and abandoned places in all mines are properly
fenced off across the openings , so that no person can
enter , and that danger signals are posted upon said
fencing to warn persons of existing danger .
Any mine in which explosive gas can be detected
with a flame safety lamp shall require the services of
a mine foreman of the first class . Any mine in which
explosive gas is not present may employ the ser-
vices of a mine foreman of the second class .
The mine foreman shall give prompt attention to
the removal of all dangers reported to him by his as-
sistants , the fire boss , or any other person working
in the mine , and in case it is impracticable to remove

192 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the danger at once , he shall notify every person
whose safety is menaced thereby to remain away
from the portion where the dangerous condition
exists . He or his assistants shall, at least once every
week , travel and examine all the air courses , roads
and openings that give access to old workings or
falls , and make a record of the condition of all places
where danger has been found , with ink , or indelible
pencil , in the record book provided for that purpose .
The mine foreman shall notify , in writing , the op-
erator or superintendent of the mine of his inability
to comply with any of the requirements of these sec-
tions , and it shall then become the duty of any oper-
ator , or superintendent , to at once attend to the mat-
ter complained of by the mine foreman so as to en-
able him to comply with the provisions thereof .
The mine foreman shall each day enter and sign
plainly with ink or indelible pencil , in a book pro-
vided for that purpose , a report of the condition of
the mine , which report shall clearly state any danger
that may come under his observation during the day,
or any danger reported to him by his assistants or
the fire bosses . The report shall also state whether
or not there is a proper supply of material on hand
for the safe working of the mine , and whether or not
the requirements of the law are complied with .
The mine foreman shall , also each day , read care-
fully, and counter sign with ink , or indelible pencil ,
all reports entered into the record book of the fire
bosses .
In case of death or resignation of a mine foreman ,
the superintendent or manager shall select a compe-
tent and certified man , if one be available and if not ,
he may temporarily select any other competent
man who may serve if approved by the district in-
spector and the Chief of the Department of Mines

CHAPTER 21 193
2
and Minerals until the next examination . He shall ,
while acting as mine foreman , be liable to the same
penalty as the mine foreman for any violations of
this Act .
It shall be the duty of the mine foreman to report
immediately all violations of this Act to the operator
or superintendent , who shall in turn report such vio-
lations to the district inspector for the district in
which the mine is situated .
The certificate of a mine foreman may be canceled
and revoked by the Board of Examiners , if it shall
be established in the judgment of said Board that
the holder thereof has become unworthy to hold said
certificate by reason of violation of law , intemperate
habits , incapacity , abuse of authority , willful negli-
gence to comply with instructions to obey the re-
quirements of the mining laws given by a district
mine inspector or other cause ; Provided , that any
person against whom charges are made shall have a
right to appear before said Board and defend
against such charges , and he shall have five days '
notice in writing of such charges previous to such
hearing .
§ 41. Explosives .-No miner or other employee
shall take into any mine in this state any larger
quantity of powder or other explosive than he may
reasonably expect to use in any one shift , and all ex-
plosives shall be carried into the mine in an insu-
lated or non -conductive , canvas , wood , fibre or other
approved receptacle of a capacity not to exceed one
shift's supply , which shall be properly closed with
an approved top . All detonators or electric squibs
shall be carried into the mine in non -conductive
wood , fibre or other approved receptacles with ap-
proved top and of a capacity not to exceed one
shift's supply . It shall be the duty of each miner to
Acts- 7

194 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
see that all explosives are separated from detona-
tors , squibs or fuses by at least twelve feet in all
working places .
No shots shall be fired in any place known to lib-
erate explosive gas until such place has been prop-
erly examined by a competent person who is desig-
nated for that purpose , and no shots shall be fired
in any place where gas is detected until said gas has
been removed by means of ventilation . After firing
any shot or shots no workmen shall return to the
working place until the smoke has cleared away ; and
before starting to work he shall make a careful ex-
amination as to the condition of the roof , and do
what is necessary to make the place safe before be-
ginning to load coal , slate or refuse .
In no case shall more than one kind of explosive
be used in the same drill hole except in strip mining
operations and every blasting hole shall be tamped ,
full from the explosive to the mouth , and no coal
dust or inflammable material shall be used for tamp-
ing . Where dynamite or keg powder is used the
method and procedure shall be approved by the dis-
trict inspector and the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals .
When fuses are used , in no case shall fuses be used
of less length than the drill hole .
No explosives or detonators shall be hauled on any
trip operated by electric haulage motors , unless en-
closed in non -conducting boxes , containers or cars
approved by the district inspector and the Chief of
the Department of Mines and Minerals .
Trips hauling explosive shall not carry workmen
other than those operating the trip , and explosives
shall not be hauled into or out of the mine within five
minutes preceding or following any trips in which
men are handled , and when traveling with the air

CHAPTER 21 195
current , the explosive trip shall precede ; if against
the air current , the man trip shall precede . Pro-
vided however , should a more safe method of trans-
porting and handling explosives or detonators be
made available after proper and sufficient tests and
experiments , the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals shall have the authority to approve the
methods to be used .
§ 42. Every miner shall thoroughly examine the
roof and general conditions of his working place
before commencing work therein and if he finds loose
rock, slate or other dangerous conditions which he
can remedy he shall take down such loose rock or
slate or securely prop same and otherwise make his
place safe before doing any other work therein.
During the progress of his work each miner shall
carefully watch his working place and if dangerous
conditions develop he shall either make same safe or
abandon the place until it is made safe . Provided ,
however , where the operator elects to do the timber-
ing it shall be the duty of the miner to forsake his
working place until the place has been made safe .
§ 43. No miner , workman or other person , shall
knowingly injure any shaft , lamp , instrument , air
course , or brattice or obstruct or throw open air-
ways , or carry matches or open lights in the places.
worked with safety lamps or electric lamps or dis-
turb any part of the machinery or appliances , open
a door used for directing ventilation and not close it
again, or enter any part of a mine against caution
or disobey any order given in carrying out any of
the provisions of this Act , or do any other act
whereby the life or health of any person employed in
the mine or the security of the mine is endangered ,
or enter or be about any mine while intoxicated or
under the influence of alcohol .

196 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
$ 44. Maps and Plans .-The operator or super-
intendent of every coal mine in this state shall annu-
ally and begin not later than five days after the
first day of January , make or cause to be made an
accurate map or plan of the workings of such mine ,
on a scale of not more than one hundred, two hun-
dred , three hundred or four hundred feet to the
inch, showing the area mined and the forms of the
excavations up to the said first day of January ,
together with the location and connection of the lines
of all adjoining lands within one thousand feet of
said excavations , and the names or name of each
owner or owners , so far as known , marked on each
tract , a true copy of which map the said operator or
superintendent shall deposit with the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals within forty-five
days after said first day of January , and another
copy of which shall be kept at the office of such mine .
But after the making and filing with said Chief of
the Department of Mines and Minerals of the map
of the mine , as required herein , the operator or su-
perintendent shall only be required to annually file
with said Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals within the time herein specified , such ad-
ditional map and statement as may be necessary to
truly show the progress of the workings , the amount
and forms of excavation of said mine , and so much
of the property lines as may be within one thousand
feet of said excavation as extended , from the date of
the preceding map or survey up to the first day of
January as provided herein . The Chief of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals shall annually , be-
fore the first day of January , give warning notice
that said map is required . Provided , however , that
if for good reason the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals deems it advisable for any op-

CHAPTER 21 197
erator or superintendent to furnish a map or exten-
sion other than required in this section , the operator
or superintendent shall do so forthwith . If the op-
erator or superintendent of any coal mine shall fail
or neglect to furnish to the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals any copy of the map or ex-
tension thereof as provided in this Act , the Chief of
the Department of Mines and Minerals is hereby au-
thorized to cause a correct survey and map or plan
of said mine or the extensions above indicated to be
made at the expense of the owner , lessee , or oper-
ator of such mine , the cost of which shall be recover-
able from said owner , lessee or operator as other
debts are recoverable by law ; and if at any time the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall have reason to believe that such map or plan ,
or the extension thereof , furnished in pursuance of
this article is materially incorrect , such will not
serve the purpose for which it was intended , he may
have survey and map of plan or the extension
thereof made or corrected , and expense of making
such survey and map or plan or extension thereof
under the direction of said Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals shall be paid by the owner ,
lessee , or operator of the mine , and the same may be
collected as other debts are recoverable by law ; but
if the map , plan or extension as furnished by the op-
erator or superintendent shall be found to be cor-
rect , the expense of said survey and drafting of map
or plan shall be paid by the state . The correctness
of each map or plan provided for by this artitle
shall be certified by the person making such map or
plan, as follows :
I, the undersigned , hereby certify that this map is
correct and shows all the information to the best of
my knowledge and belief required by the mining

198 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
laws of this state , and covers the period ending
Engineer .
day of
Acknowledged before me , a
this .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals may reject any map as incomplete the accu-
racy of which is not so attested . The Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals shall, upon the
application of any owner , lessee , or operator there-
for , make or cause to be made a duplicate of any
map on file in his office of any mine owned , leased or
operated by the owner , lessee , or operator making
such application , but the cost of making such dupli-
cate shall be paid by the applicant therefor . In no
case shall any copy of such map be made without the
consent of such owner , lessee , or operator , except
that in any case where the owner or owners of any
land adjacent or near to the land of the owner , les-
see , or operator , may file an affidavit with the Chief '
of the Department of Mines and Minerals showing
ownership of such land adjacent or near to the land
of such owner , lessee , or operator , and that he verily
believes that such owner , lessee or operator is en-
croaching or mining on his or their land or lands ,
whereupon it shall be the duty of the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals to cause to be
furnished to such affiant or affiants a duplicate of
map filed by such encroaching owner , operator or
lessee , the cost of which duplicate shall be paid by
the affiant or a ; ants . Wwhen any coal mine is worked
out or is about to be abandoned , or indefinitely
closed , the operator of the same shall make or cause
to be made , a final survey of such mine , to show the

CHAPTER 21 199
entire worked-out area at the time the mine was
closed , and the results shall be duly extended on the
map of the mine previously made , and a copy thereof
filed with the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals .
§ 45. Adjoining Property Owners .-No owner ,
or tenant of any land containing coal shall open or
sink or dig , excavate or work in any coal mine or
shaft on such land , within five feet of the land divid-
ing said land from that of another person or per-
sons , without the consent , in writing , of every per-
son interested in or having title to , such adjoining
land in possession , revision , or remainder , or the
guardians of any such persons as may be infants ,
nor without in addition thereto the approval of the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals . If
any person shall violate this section , he shall be lia-
ble to the person or persons injured thereby , who
may sue for same , in the sum of five hundred dol-
lars ; provided , that nothing in this act shall deprive
such injured person or persons from pursuing any
and all other remedies now provided or that may
hereafter be provided by law for recovery or dam-
ages for injuries done to their property .
§ 46. Coterminous Land Owners .- The owner ,
tenant or occupant of any land on which a coal mine
is opened and worked , or his superintendent shall
permit any person interested in, or having title to ,
any land coterminous with that in which such coal
mine is , to have ingress and egress with surveyors
and assistants to explore and survey such mine at
his own expense , and not oftener than once a month
for the purpose of ascertaining whether or not the
preceding section has been violated . Every owner ,
tenant , occupant or superintendent who shall refuse
such permission , exploration , or survey shall be lia-

200 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ble to the person so refused in the sum of twenty
dollars for each refusal , for which the person so re-
fused may maintain an action as provided by law .
The judge of the county in which such mine is lo-
cated , before whom complaint of such refusal is
made , shall issue a summons to such owner , tenant ,
occupant or superintendent to answer such com-
plaint . On the return of the summons executed , and
proof that the complainant has right of entry, and
that he has been refused without sufficient cause , the
judge shall designate an early and convenient time
for such entry to be made , and issue his warrant
commanding the sheriff of the county to attend and
prevent obstructions and impediments to such entry ,
exploration and survey. The cost of such summons ,
and a fee of three dollars to the sheriff executing the
warrant shall be paid by the person whose refusal
caused the complaint . But if the judge dismisses
the complaint , the cost shall be paid by the party
making it ; provided however , that either party may
have the right of appeal as in other actions .
§ 47. Regulations for Storage of Oils .- All
buildings used for the storage of oils or similar in-
flammable material shall be located not within one
hundred feet of any hoisting or escape shaft or mine
opening . Oil or grease shall be taken into the mine
from day to day with one day's supply being the
maximum .
$ 48. Board of Examiners .-The Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals and any two of
the district inspectors , the selection of whom is op-
tional with the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals and who may select them alternately
from time to time , together with one miner holding
mine foreman's certificate of first class , and who has
had at least seven (7 ) years of practical experience

CHAPTER 21 201
in the mining of coal , four ( 4 ) years of which must
have been in the mines in this state and who is act-
ually employed in mining coal at the time of appoint-
ment , and one coal operator who is at the time of the
appointment actually engaged in the position of op-
erating a coal mine in the state , and who shall be
recommended by the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals , subject to the approval and ap-
pointment by the Governor for a term of four years
each shall constitute the Board of Examiners for the
application for the certificate of qualifications to
serve as district mine inspector , mine foreman , and
fire boss in coal mines .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Miner-
als shall be chairman of the Board and whenever it
may be necessary for him to be absent from any
meeting of the Board , he may designate any other
member to preside during his absence . The major-
ity of the members shall constitute a quorum , set
authority to transact business , hold examinations
and issue certificates .
To all persons possessing the qualifications re-
quired by this certificate for appointment as district
mine inspector , mine foreman and fire boss and who
have passed a satisfactory examination before the
Board of Examiners a certificate shall be given in
such form as may be prescribed by the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals certifying that
said applicant has passed a satisfactory examina-
tion and possesses qualifications by this Act re-
quired of the district mine inspector , mine foreman ,
and fire boss . Such certificate shall be signed by the
members of the Board of Examiners .
$ 49. Examination Fee and Certificates.-
Before taking the examination for district mine in-
spector , mine foreman or fire boss each applicate for

202 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the examination shall, before he is examined , pay a
fee of two dollars and fifty cents ( $ 2.50 ) to the Au-
ditor of Public Accounts of this state , who
shall issue him receipt therefor , which receipt the
applicant shall present to the Board of Examiners
before he shall be permitted to enter the examina-
tion and all fees so paid shall be turned into the
treasury to the credit of the State Department of
Mines and Minerals .
The certificates to be awarded successful appli-
cant for a certificate of qualification to serve as dis-
trict mine inspector shall show upon its face that the
holder is authorized to act as district mine inspector .
The class of certificate which may be awarded an
applicant who has been examined with respect to his
qualifications to serve as mine foreman shall be de-
termined by the Board of Examiners according to
the nature of the examination taken by the appli-
cant .
The certificate to be awarded a successful appli-
cant for a certificate of qualification to serve as fire
boss shall show upon its face that the holder is au-
thorized to act as fire boss .
No certificate shall be granted to any person who
does not present to the Board of Examiners satis-
factory evidence , in the form of affidavits made by
well known and responsible persons in the locality.
whence he came , that the applicant is a man of sobri-
ety and good moral character , and that he has
had at least five (5 ) years practical experience at
and in coal mines . Certificates may be granted only
to persons who are bona fide residents of this state ,
or who are employed at mines within the state . The
Board is authorized to decline to examine any appli-
cant who can not readily understand the written

CHAPTER 21 203
English language or who can not express himself in-
telligently in the English language .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals , as chairman , shall keep a record of all pro-
ceedings of the Board , including the names and ad-
dresses of persons who have been granted certifi-
cates and of those who have failed to pass the exam-
ination ,
Provided however , that the passage of this Act
shall in no wise effect the certification of those per-
sons already possessing certificates of qualification
to act as mine inspector , mine foreman or fire boss
respectively .
If any district mine inspector, mine foreman or
fire boss should happen to have destroyed or mis-
placed his certificate of competency to act as district
mine inspector, mine foreman or fire boss he shall
apply to the Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals for a duplicate certificate . Included with
the application must be an affidavit stating in full
how the certificate was destroyed or misplaced and
if said Chief of the Department of Mines and Miner-
als shall deem it proper he shall furnish said dis-
trict mine inspector , mine foreman or fire boss with
a duplicate certificate , provided , however , that the
person making request for a duplicate certificate
shall pay a fee of two dollars ( $2.00 ) to the Auditor
of Public Accounts of this state .
$ 50. Duties of Inspector of Mine Weights .— It
shall be the duty of the Inspector of Mine Weights
in this Act provided for to inspect and test all scales
used in connection with any commercial coal mine in
this state ( railroad scales excepted ) . Said Inspec-
tor shall determine the mechanical condition and ac-
curacy of said scales . It shall be the duty of any
coal operator or superintendent to render such as-

204 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sistance to the Inspector of Mine Weights as may be
necessary in conveying and handling the weights
and other appliances that may be needed for the
proper inspecting and testing of said scales . The
Inspector of Mine Weights shall leave a written.
record with the mine operator or superintendent as
to the condition of the scales inspected and shall
post a record of such inspection in the weight office
of said scales . If upon inspecting and testing any
mine scale the inspector deems it necessary that the
said scale should have any part or parts replaced
with new parts or if the scale is found to need a re-
conditioning or the foundation or building repaired
or renewed it shall be the duty of the operator or
superintendent to comply with the recommendations
forthwith .
$ 51 . Mine Scales .-The operator or superin-
tendent of all commercial coal mines in this state
shall provide at such mine tipple or dumping point
suitable and accurate scales for the weighing of coal
for which the miners are to be paid , and when dif-
ferences arise between the operator or superintend-
ent of the mine and the miners employed in the same
as to the accuracy or capacity of the scales and if
the matter cannot be adjusted locally , the question
shall be referred to the State Department of Mines
and Minerals whose duty it shall be to inspect and
test said scales as early as practicable after receiv-
ing notification ; and should said inspector find the
scales inaccurate or defective beyond the limit ad-
mitted in such scales by the State Department of
Mines and Minerals he shall notify the operator or
superintendent of the mine , and said scales shall
forthwith be repaired and made accurate , or accu-
rate scales be substituted therefor . If however , after
inspecting and testing the scales the Inspector shall

CHAPTER 21 205
find the scales to be weighing within the limit as ad-
mitted by the State Department of Mines and Min-
erals , the Inspector will so advise the representative
of the miners and the operator or superintendent in
a manner prescribed by the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals . Provided however , where
coal is loaded by mechanical means by the day or by
the car the above provision shall not apply . Other
than to adjust the tare weights for the different
types of mine cars and balance the scales , no scale
shall be tampered with in any manner by any person
whatsoever .
§ 52. Check Weighman .- That when a majority
of the miners engaged in digging or mining coal in
any coal mine in this state , request the owner or
owners , or operator of said mines to allow said
miners to employ , at their own expense , a person to
inspect the weights at said mine , and see that all the
coal digged and mined by said miner is properly
weighed and accounted for , and do and perform only
such other duties as will insure that said coal is
properly weighed and correctly accounted for , said
owner or owners or operator or operators shall per-
mit such person to be employed by said miners mak-
ing the request . Provided , the person so employed
has the reputation of being an honest , trustworthy ,
discreet , sober and upright man . Said check weigh-
man shall be duly elected by a majority of the em-
ployees engaged in mining and loading coal and said
election shall be properly conducted by secret ballot
at the principal entrance to the mine .
The appointment under the provisions of this Act
of each check weighman , shall be approved by the
County Judge of the County on presentation of an
affidavit stating that said person has been duly and

206 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
properly elected in accordance with the provisions
of this Act .
The person appointed and employed by the min-
ers to perform the duties set forth in this Act
shall have free access to the mine scales while the
mine is in operation or the scales are being tested
and he shall hold no other office , nor have any duties
other than check weighman . The said person so em-
ployed by the miners shall not be hindered or pre-
vented from a proper performance of his duties by
the person who weighs coal for the operator or op-
erators of any mine or mines , nor any of the agents
or employees of said operator or operators .
person employed by the miners shall in no way pre-
vent the weighman or other employees of said oper-
ator or operators from performing their duties in a
proper manner .
Said
The operator or superintendent shall see that cars
are tared at reasonable intervals and said tare to be
taken of the cars in their usual running condition .
If a suitable person is not available among the em-
ployees of the mine to act as check weighman , then
a man not employed at the mine may be selected as
check weighman upon mutual agreement between the
miners and the operator or operators of said mine
or mines .
$ 53. Mine Rules.-The operator of any coal
mine to which this act applies shall have the right to
adopt special rules for the government and opera-
tion of his mine or mines not in conflict with the pro-
visions of this Act covering all the work pertaining
thereto in and out of the mine , but before said rules
shall be in effect they shall be approved in writing
by the Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals . Such rules when so adopted and approved in
writing by the Chief of the Department of Mines and

CHAPTER 21 207
Minerals shall be printed on cardboard in the lan-
guage spoken by seventy -five per cent (75% ) or
more employees at such mine or mines , and shall be
posted on a bulletin board provided for that purpose
or some other conspicuous place about the mine
where the same may be seen and observed by the
employees at such mine ; and when said rules are
so adopted and approved by the Chief of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals and posted all em-
ployees at such mine shall be deemed to have notice
thereof, and to have agreed to the provisions
thereof, and any persons who shall violate the pro-
