visions of this Act or said Mine Rules shall be guilty
of a misdemeanor and upon conviction thereof suf-
fer the penalties as hereinafter provided .
It shall be the duty of such mine operator when so
requested by an employee to furnish a printed copy
of such rules to the employee so requesting the
same . Said rules to be printed in a form prescribed
by the Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals.
It shall be the duty of any person employed in or
about the coal mines of this state to obey the mining
law and the mine rules and in case of failure to obey
such law and rules , he shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor and upon conviction suffer the penalties as
hereinafter provided .
$ 54. Workmen's Compensation . The person-
nel of the Department of Mines and Minerals shall
be placed under the provisions of the Workmen's
Compensation laws of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky .

56. Each coal operator as defined herein shall

CHAPTER 22 .
AN ACT to regulate , control and fix standard weights of wheat ,
flour , label and the size of packages containing same ; and
to provide penalties for the violation of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
State of Kentucky :
$ 1. That the standard measure of wheat flour
shall be packages containing net avoirdupois
weights of two ( 2 ) , six ( 6 ) , twelve ( 12 ) , twenty -four
( 24 ) , forty -eight ( 48 ) , ninety -six ( 96 ) and one hun-
dred and ninety-six ( 196 ) pounds .

212 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
$ 2. Be it further enacted that it shall be un-
lawful for any person , partnership or corporation
to pack for sale , sell or offer for sale in Kentucky
any wheat flour except in bags or packages con-
taining the above standard net weights .
3. Each package shall have the net weight ,
name and address of manufacturer printed or plain-
ly marked on it in letters and figures not less than
one -half ( 2 ) inch in height .
§ 4. Provided , however , that the provisions of
this Act shall not apply to the retailing of flour di-
rect to the consumer from bulk stock or to the
bakery trade or to the exchanging of wheat for flour
by mills grinding for toll and that nothing herein
contained shall be held to apply to any wheat
product packed and distributed as a specialty in
identified original package , the net contents of which
weight is five (5 ) pounds or less .
§ 5. Be it further enacted , that any violation of
this Act shall be a misdemeanor and upon conviction ,
the offender shall be fined not less than Twenty -five
($25.00 ) Dollars nor more than Five Hundred
($ 500.00 ) Dollars for each offense .
§ 6. Be it further enacted , that all acts or parts
of acts in conflict herewith be repealed .
§ 7. Be it further enacted , that this Act shall
take effect immediately upon its passage ; the public
welfare requiring it.
Approved June 21 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 23 .
AN ACT providing for rules and regulations for the operation
of nudist colonies in this Commonwealth ; providing for the
payment of a license fee ; providing for penalty for the viola-
tion of this Act ; repealing all laws in conflict.

CHAPTER 23 213
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Nudists , as defined in this Act , means any
person or group of persons who display any part of
their private person naked before persons of the
opposite sex , not their husband or wife , at their
solicitation or with their consent for religious or
health purposes.
§ 2. Hereafter , any person , firm , corporation or
organization desiring to own or operate a nudist
colony must first secure a license therefor from the
State Tax Commission , and pay an annual tax there-
on of One Thousand ($1,000.00 ) Dollars .
§ 3. In order that the privacy of the person or
persons living in a nudist colony might be assured ,
it shall be unlawful for any person , firm , corporation
or other organization to operate said colony without
first building a wall twenty feet in height , made of
brick , stone or cement around the premises on which
the colony is located .
4. It shall be the duty of the owner or opera-
tor or person in charge of such colony to keep a
register in which shall be kept the names and ad-
dresses of all persons who may enter said colony ,
which register shall be open to inspection at all times
to the proper authorities .
§ 5. Said colony shall be inspected by such per-
sons and at such times as may be deemed necessary
by the Attorney General .
§ 6. Any person , firm , corporation or organiza-
tion found guilty of violating the provisions of this
Act shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and
upon conviction shall be fined not less than One
Thousand ($1,000.00 ) Dollars .
7. All laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

214 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 24.
AN ACT providing for the consolidation of the office of jailer
with that of sheriff in each county of the State .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. The office of jailer is hereby consolidated
with that of sheriff in each county of the State ,
under the provisions of Section 105 of the Constitu-
tion . There are hereby transferred to and vested
in the sheriff , all the powers and duties heretofore
authorized by law to be exercised or performed by
the jailer . Wherever in any law of the State , refer-
ence is made to the jailer , such reference shall be
deemed to apply to the sheriff , except where the
context requires otherwise .
§ 2. All laws and parts of laws in conflict with
this act , are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict .
§ 3. This act shall take effect at the expiration
of the term of the jailers in office when the act is
passed . Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 25 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , imposing an excise
tax on the gross receipts of certain persons , firms , corpora-
tions , associations , co-partnerships and other forms of busi-
ness organizations engaged in selling at retail certain types
of personal property and /or certain classes of services and /or
entertainment ; providing for the enforcement of said act , the
collection and distribution of the revenue arising therefrom
and repealing all conflicting laws , and declaring an emergency
to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. This act shall be known and may be cited
as the Gross Receipts Tax Law of 1934.

CHAPTER 25 215
§ 2. The term " merchant " is hereby defined for
purposes of this act as a person regularly engaged
in the vending of tangible personal property .
The term " person " is hereby defined for pur-
poses of this act as including any individual , co-
partnership , association , corporation , trust or other
form of business organization , regardless of whether
or not enumerated here and regardless of whether
or not similar to those enumerated . Two or more
business organizations held by majority stock owner-
ship or ultimately controlled or directed by one man-
agement or association shall be construed for pur-
poses of this act as one person .
The term " sales at retail " is hereby defined for
the purposes of this Act as sales of tangible personal
property not intended for resale . The term , how-
ever , does not include casual sales by a person not
regularly engaged in making sales , sales of material
which enters into and becomes a part of a finished
product to be offered for sale , including the con-
tainer in which such product is sold ; sales by farm-
ers of commodities which they or their tenants or
employees have produced on their farms , or sales
of coal by the producers in quantities of not less
than forty tons , sales of newspapers or of the space
therein , and retail sales of gasoline , and sales of
time on the air by radio broadcasting stations .
The term " gross receipts " when applied to mer-
chants making sales at retail shall be construed to
mean the amount received in money , credits , prop-
erty, or other money's worth , regardless of whether
or not the form thereof be enumerated herein , in
consideration of sales at retail within this State ;
provided that in case of sales on open account or
note the consideration shall only be deemed to be
gross receipts when payments on such account or
note shall be made in cash , property , or other

216 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
money's worth , regardless of whether or not the
form be enumerated in this paragraph ; provided ,
further , that credits for returned or defective goods
and cash discounts allowed and taken on sales shall
not be included as gross receipts , or if included and
later allowed or credited , may be deducted from
gross receipts in the month in which allowed or
credited . Gross receipts , as thus defined , shall be
deemed to include receipts by a merchant operating
a place of business or store , irrespective of whether
or not the amounts be received for sales made at the
store and receipts by merchants operating a store
and/or wagons or trucks or any other conveyance
from which tangible personal property is sold and
delivered , and receipts from goods , wares , sold by
sample and later delivered , and from the sale of
other commodities not at the place of business or
store , regardless of whether or not mentioned here
and whether or not similar to these , shall be includ-
ed. As applied to places of amusement and/or en-
tertainment, the term shall be construed to mean the
amount received in money , credits , property , or
other money's worth , regardless of whether or not
the form thereof be enumerated herein , in consid-
eration of admissions , operation of machines , exer-
cise of privileges granted for a charge , rentals on
property , advertising by the said places of amuse-
ment and/or entertainment , or other accounts re-
ceived by said places , regardless of form and re-
gardless of whether or not enumerated in this para-
graph and whether or not similar to those enumer-
ated .
The term " places of amusement and/or enter-
tainment " is hereby defined for purposes of this act
as including hotels and restaurants ( as defined in
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , Section
2059 ) , theatres , race courses , dance halls , skating

CHAPTER 25 217
rinks , pool rooms , ball parks and other athletic ex-
hibits or contests , bowling alleys , circuses , carni-
vals , amusement parks , swimming pools , and other
places , regardless of whether or not enumerated in
this paragraph and whether or not similar to those
enumerated herein, which make a charge for admis-
sion or for other services and which are advertised
or otherwise represented as offering their facilities
to the public or to a section thereof.
The term " gross receipts " when applied to per-
sons engaged in the sale and/or transportation of
electric current , water, artificial or natural gas , or
persons engaged in furnishing ways and means for
the transmission of the voice and/or messages of
persons to a person or persons within the Common-
wealth , shall be construed to mean the amount re-
ceived in money , credits , property , or other money's
worth , regardless of whether or not the form there-
of be enumerated herein ; provided , that in case the
business is conducted on open account or on note ,
the consideration shall only be deemed to be gross
receipts when payments on such account or note shall
be made in cash , property or other money's worth
regardless of whether or not the form be enumerated
herein .
The term " retailer " is hereby defined for pur
poses of this act as a person making sales at retail .
The term " manufacturer " is hereby defined as a
person engaged in manufacturing (as the term is
used in Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition ,
Section 4019a- 10) .
"
The term " jobber ' is hereby defined for purposes
of this act as one, not a manufacturer , regularly en
gaged in making sales of personal property intended
for resale .
The terms " Commission , " " Tax Commission , "
and " State Tax Commission " are each to be con-

218 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
strued for purposes of this act as meaning the Ken-
tucky State Tax Commission .
The terms " store " is hereby defined for purposes
of this act as a building , room , or place in or at
which tangible personal property is kept for sale ,
or from or at which such property is sold .
3a . An excise tax is hereby imposed on every
merchant engaged in the sale of tangible personal
property at retail , equal to three per centum (3% )
of the aggregate gross receipts from the sale of
tangible personal property by merchants made
after this act becomes effective , and every merchant
engaged in the sale of tangible personal property
at retail is required to collect the tax from the pur-
chaser and/or account to the State of Kentucky for
such tax, in the manner provided in this act .
$ 3b . An excise tax is hereby imposed on every
person operating a place of amusement and/or en-
tertainment equal to three per centum ( 3 % ) of the
gross receipts derived from the operation of said
place or places of amusement and/or entertainment ,
and every person operating a place of amusement
and/or entertainment is required to collect a tax
from the purchaser of the tickets of admission
and/or account to the State of Kentucky for such
tax , in the manner provided in this act .
§ 3c . An excise tax is hereby imposed on every
person engaged in the sale and/or transportation
of electric current , water , artificial or natural gas ,
or persons engaged in furnishing the ways or means
for the transmission of the voice and /or messages
of persons to person or persons within the Common-
wealth , excepting the United States mail , equal to
three per centum (3 % ) of the aggregate gross re-
ceipts of such persons so engaged in such respective
businesses , and such person is required to collect
the tax from the purchaser and/or account to the

CHAPTER 25 219
State of Kentucky for such tax in the manner pro-
vided in this act ; provided , however , that no tax
shall be imposed upon sales of electric current , water,
artificial or natural gas to the United States or to
any State or Territory or any political subdivision
thereof and provided further that no tax shall be
imposed upon sales of electric current , water , arti-
ficial or natural gas for resale or for use in manu-
facturing , processing , mining or refining .
§ 4. Every person , whose gross receipts are
made taxable under the provisions of this act , shall
deduct from the amount collected and/or due as
tax under the provisions of this act , three per centum
(3% ) of the amount reported each month , to cover
the expenses incident to the reporting and remitting
of such tax as provided under this act , or as may
be provided by rules and regulations adopted by the
Tax Commission pursuant to the provisions of this
act .
5. Every charitable and educational institution
in the State of Kentucky and institutions thereof
when engaged in activities made taxable by this act
shall be completely exempt from its provisions .
§ 6. It is hereby declared to be the legislative
intent that every person engaged in the various busi-
ness enterprises designated in this act shall be made
taxable under this act on account of gross receipts
of such business . The Commission shall prescribe
appropriate rules and regulations to make this sec-
tion effective .
$ 7. The State Tax Commission shall promulgate
rules and regulations for the various classes of busi
ness or enterprises , whose gross receipts are made
taxable by this Act , which rules and regulations
shall set forth the manner whereby the taxes shall
be added to the retail price of the various articles ,
commodities or services , and , for the purpose of

220 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
auditing , the Tax Commission may require the keep-
ing and filing of sales tickets showing the amount
of the tax added to the sales of said articles , com-
modities or services .
In order to carry out the provisions of this section ,
it shall be unlawful for any person to advertise ,
either orally or in writing , that he is paying said
taxes from any source other than the receipts of his
business , and it shall be unlawful to advertise any
article or service taxable hereunder as being sold.
free of the tax herein imposed .
8. Every person operating a business made
taxable under the provisions of this act on gross
receipts , shall , on or before the 15th day of the
month succeeding the month in which this bill be-
comes a law , file with the Tax Commission an appli-
cation for a license containing the person's name ,
the address , or addresses , of his place , or places , of
business , and such additional data as the Tax Com-
mission may require . Upon filing said name , ad-
dress and additional data , the Commission shall is-
sue a license to do business in Kentucky . It is
hereby made a misdemeanor to engage or continue
in business as a person operating a business made
taxable under the provisions of this act on gross
receipts without obtaining and continually display-
ing prominently in or at said person's place of busi-
ness , the license herein authorized to be issued by the
State Tax Commission . In the case of a person
having more than one place of business or store , or
in the case of a person operating two or more places
of amusement and/or entertainment , the Tax Com-
mission shall issue a sufficient number of copies of
the license so that one may be displayed in each
place of business operated by such person .
§ 9. The administration of this act is vested in
the State Tax Commission , which may prescribe

CHAPTER 25 221
forms and promulgate rules and regulations needed
for the enforcement of this act .
The State Tax Commission or any representative
appointed by it in writing , is hereby authorized to
examine the books , records , papers , files , equipment ,
and/or inventory of any person , made taxable on
his gross receipts under the provisions of this act ;
to examine witnesses under oath for purposes in-
volving enforcement of the act ; and tò examine the
books , records , papers , files , equipment and/or in-
ventory of manufacturers , jobbers , retailers , or car-
riers for the purpose of determining whether the
act is being violated . Provided that the sheriffs of
the State are hereby made the local agents of the
State Tax Commission in their respective counties
of the State for the purpose of supervising the pay-
ment and collection of the Sales Tax and checking
and examining the accounts and records of mer-
chants but the State Tax Commission may upon
request of any sheriff , when complications and dif-
ficulties arise , send its special agent into any county
to aid and assist the sheriff and this Act shall not
authorize the said Tax Commission to appoint ad-
ditional field agents or other employees without the
approval of the Governor .
The sheriffs of the various counties shall be al-
lowed two per centum ( 2 % ) of the total tax collected
and reported by the county to the State Tax Com-
mission , provided that no sheriff shall receive less
than One Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) nor more
than Two Thousand Dollars ( $2,000.00 ) per year
for his services connected with this Act . The sheriff
shall be paid in two installments each year on Janu-
ary 1 and July 1. The State Tax Commission shall
certify the amount due each sheriff to the Auditor
of Public Accounts and he to the Treasurer for pay-
ment out of the funds accruing under this act and

222 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
this commission to the sheriffs is intended to repay
them for losses sustained by them under the recent
real estate reduction act, but the total salary of any
sheriff in this Commonwealth shall not exceed Five
Thousand Dollars ( $ 5,000.00 ) annually .
The State Tax Commission shall have the power
to institute a legal proceeding by the Attorney Gen-
eral of the State of Kentucky , or any attorney duly
authorized by him to do so , for the purpose of ascer ·
taining the amount of tax due and of enforcing the
collection thereof with penalties thereon and for the
purpose of enjoining the operation of the business
of the delinquent person , operating a business made
taxable under this act on its gross receipts until said
tax is paid .
The Commission may at or after the commence-
ment of an action for the purpose of collecting the
amount of tax due and penalty and interest thereon
as set out herein have an attachment against the
property of distributor or garnishment of his cred-
itors without the execution of bond , upon filing an
affidavit showing the nature of the claim , that it is
just, the sum which the Commission believes it
should recover and an indication that it believes col-
lection of the demand will be endangered by delay in
obtaining judgment or by a return of " no property
found ."
Any information obtained by the State Tax Com-
mission , or any agent or representative thereof, as
a result of the reports , investigations , or verifica-
tions herein authorized and required to be made ,
shall be confidential as respecting the business of
any person, operating a business made taxable
under this act on its gross receipts , and any person
divulging such information , except on the order of
a court of competent jurisdiction , or to an officer
of the Commonwealth entitled to receive the in-

CHAPTER 25 223
formation in his official capacity , shall be fined not
exceeding five hundred dollars ( $ 500.00 ) ; provided ,
however , that this provision shall not be construed
to prevent the State Tax Commission's publishing
statistics based on information thus secured , in such
a manner as not to reveal data respecting the busi-
ness of any particular person operating a business
made taxable under the provisions of this act on
gross receipts .
The State Tax Commission shall keep a suitable
record of all taxes collected under this act , which
record shall include preservation of the original re-
turns by tax payers for a period of not less than
three (3 ) years .
§ 10. Every person operating a business made
taxable under this act on the gross receipts is here-
by required to preserve a written record of daily
gross receipts , to maintain a file of invoices of goods
received for sale , and to keep such other records
as the Commission may require to facilitate en-
forcement of this act . On or before the 15th day
of the month following the end of each month , be-
ginning with the month after which this bill becomes
a law , each retailer and each person operating a
place of amusement and/or entertainment or any
other business made taxable under the provisions
of this act , shall mail to , or file with the Commission
a verified written report of total gross receipts
made during the preceding month , and shall remit
with said report the amount of tax due under Sec-
tions 3 and 4 of this act ; provided , however , that the
Tax Commission , on the basis of information herein
authorized to be secured , or on the basis of an audit
of the return , may require additional payments if
found to be due .
§ 11. No suit shall be maintained in any court
to restrain or delay the collection or payment of the

224 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tax levied by this act . The aggrieved tax payer
shall pay the tax as and when required , and may ,
at any time within two years from the date of such
payment , sue the Commonwealth through its agent ,
the Auditor of Public Accounts , in an action at law
in any court, State or Federal , otherwise having
jurisdiction of the parties and subject matter , for
the recovery of the tax paid with legal interest there-
on from the date of payment .
If it is finally determined that said tax , or any
part thereof , was wrongfully collected , for any
reason , it shall be the duty of the Auditor of Public
Accounts to issue his warrant on the Treasurer of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the amount of
such tax so adjudged to have been wrongfully col-
lected together with legal interest thereon . The
Treasurer shall pay the same at once out of the
general expenditure fund of the State in preference
to other warrants or claims against the Common-
wealth . A separate suit need not be filed for each
indivdual payment made by any tax payer , but a
recovery may be had in one suit for as many pay-
ments as may have been made .
§ 12. If gross receipts made taxable by this act
are received under such circumstances that the Con-
stitution and laws of the United States , or the Con-
stitution of Kentucky , preclude imposition of the
tax, then said tax shall not apply .
13. Any person who shall violate any of the
provisions of this act for which a specific penalty
is not herein provided shall be deemed guilty of a
misdemeanor and punished by a fine of not less than
$5.00 nor more than five hundred dollars ( $ 500.00 )
for each offense .
Any person , required under this act to make re-
turns and pay a tax, who shall fail to pay the tax
due in the time specified in this act , shall be subject

CHAPTER 25 225
to a penalty equal to twenty per centum ( 20 % ) of
the amount of tax due and delinquent and , in addi-
tion thereto , interest at six per centum ( 6% ) per
annum .
§ 14. The aggregate of the total of the revenue
collected from the operation of this act shall be cov-
ered through the Auditor of Public Accounts into
the State Treasury , and two -thirds thereof shall be
credited to the general expenditure fund . The re-
maining one-third shall, by the Auditor of Public
Accounts , on or before the 15th day of the second
month following the end of each quarter , be credited
to a fund to be known as the " County Road Fund , "
and shall distribute said fund immediately to the
several counties of the Commonwealth in the fol-
iowing manner :
One -third of said County Road Fund shall be dis-
tributed equally among the one hundred and twenty
counties of the State . One -third of the said County
Road Fund shall be distributed to the one hundred
and twenty counties of the State , on a basis of popu-
lation ; that is to say that each county shall receive
that proportion of said fund which its population
bears to the population of the State . In determining
the population of the State and the several counties ,
the Auditor shall use as a basis the official United
States census of 1930. One -third of the said County
Road Fund shall be distributed to the one hundred
and twenty counties of the State , on a basis of square
mile area ; that is to say that each county shall re-
ceive that proportion of said fund which its square
mile area bears to the square mile area of Kentucky .
The treasurers of the several counties of the
Commonwealth shall receive and accept the funds
so transmitted to them and credit them to the road
fund of their respective counties . The fiscal courts
shall first direct the payment annually from the
Acts- 8

226 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
road fund to the sinking fund , all of such fund so
received under this act , or such a sum which is suf-
ficient to make the payments upon principal and in-
terest of the bonded indebtedness of such county
created by the construction and/or maintenance of
public roads , or bridges, or , if there be no bonded in-
debtedness , then all of the fund so received under
the provisions of this act shall be applied to the
payment of the floating indebtedness created by the
construction and/or maintenance of public roads and
bridges in the county , and , if there be no bonded or
floating indebtedness created by the construction
and/or maintenance of public roads and bridges
within the counties , then such funds so received by
the provisions of this act shall be expended under
the orders of the fiscal courts for the construction
and/or maintenance of public roads within the
counties .
§ 15. This act shall automatically expire at mid-
night June 30 , 1936 .
§ 16. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with
this act are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict , but it is hereby declared to be the legisla-
tive intent not to repeal Chapter 149 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1930 , now Sections 4202a-
1 to 4202a -12 , inclusive , of Baldwin's Kentucky
Statute Service of 1933 , known as the Gross Sales
Act of 1930 , unless it should be determined by the
court of last resort that this act could not be made
effective unless said Chapter 149 be first repealed .
If it should be determined by the Courts that said
Chapter 149 is repealed by this Act , it is hereby de-
clared to be the legislative intent and is expressly
provided that the administrative provisions of said
Act shall remain in effect until all revenues accruing
under said Act have been collected and all actions
involving the collection of taxes accruing under said

CHAPTER 26 227
Act shall be maintained , prosecuted and defended by
the Commonwealth until said taxes have been col-
lected ,
§ 17. It is hereby declared to be the legislative
intent that the provisions of this act are severable ,
and if any of its provisions , or the application there-
of, shall be held unconstitutional , the decisions of
the court shall not effect or impair any of the re-
maining provisions of this act , it being the legisla-
tive intent that this act would have been adopted
had not such unconstitutional provisions been en-
acted therein .
§ 18. Whereas , the present State revenue does
not meet the immediate needs of the maintenance of
the State government , its agencies and subdivisions
and the State's credit is threatened with impair .
ment , an emergency is declared to exist ; and this act
shall become a law and be effective upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .

CHAPTER 26 .
AN ACT defining the word "merchant " ; imposing an excise or
license tax on merchants ; providing for the administration
and collection of such tax ; placing the tax in the general
fund ; fixing fines and penalties for the violation of the Act ;
repealing Chapter 149 of the Acts of the 1930 General As-
sembly , and providing that the repeal of said Act shall not
affect the collection and payment of any tax accruing under
said Act prior to the passage of this Act ; declaring an
emergency to exist ; and repealing all laws in conflict with
the provisions of this Act.

228 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The word " merchant , " as used in this Act ,
shall mean and include every person , association , co-
partnership or corporation engaged in the buying
and selling of goods , wares or merchandise at any
store , stand or place in this State occupied for that
purpose . In determining the number of stores ,
stands or places of business owned or operated by
a merchant for the purpose of fixing the tax due , the
merchant having a controlling interest , directly or
indirectly , in any store , stand or place of business
owned or operated by a merchant having a con-
trolling interest , directly or indirectly , in any store ,
stand or place of business shall be liable for and
pay the tax due . Should two or more merchants , as
defined herein , own , operate or have a controlling
interest in different stores , stands or places of busi-
ness and pool their buying or selling, each and every
store , stand or place of business , so operated , shall
be treated as being owned by one merchant for the
purpose of determining the tax due.
Provided , however , the words " store , or stands
or places of business " shall not include " filling or
service stations " maintained for the servicing of
motor vehicles if at least seventy ( 70 % ) per cent of
the business done by any person , association , co-
partnership or corporation at his or its such filling
or service station and/or stations in this State are
sales of motor fuels and/or motor lubricants .
§ 2. All taxes collected under the provisions of
this Act shall be covered through the Auditor of
Public Accounts into the State Treasury , and de-
posited in the general expenditure fund .
$ 3. Every merchant owning , operating or con-
trolling any store or stores , stands or places of busi-
ness in this State , shall, on or before July 1 , 1934 ,

CHAPTER 26 229
and annually thereafter , file with the State Tax
Commission a statement , under oath or affirmation ,
on such forms as the Commission may prescribe ,
giving the number and location of each separate
store , stand or place of business , and pay to the
Auditor of Public Accounts the license or excise tax
due under the provisions of this Act .
§ 4. Every merchant , beginning the operation
of any store , stand or place of business in this State
on any other date than July first of any year , shall
first pay the tax in the manner herein provided , and
the amount due shall be based upon that proportion
of the annual tax that the time remaining between
the date of the beginning of the operation and the
following July first bears to a calendar year .
§ 5. Every merchant establishing , operating or
maintaining one or more stores , stands or places of
business within this State , shall pay the license fee
hereinafter prescribed for the privilege of opening ,
establishing, operating or maintaining such stores ,
stands or places of business . The license fees here-
in prescribed shall be as follows :
(1 ) Upon one store the annual license fee shall
be two ( $ 2.00 ) dollars for each such store .
(2 ) Upon two stores or more , but not to exceed
ten stores , the annual license fee shall be Twenty
($20.00 ) Dollars for each such additional store .
(3 ) Upon each store in excess of ten , but not to
exceed twenty -five , the annual license fee shall be
Fifty ( $ 50.00 ) Dollars for each such additional store .
(4 ) Upon each store in excess of twenty -five , but
not to exceed fifty, the annual license fee shall be
One Hundred ( $ 100.00 ) Dollars for each such addi-
tional store .
(5 ) Upon each store in excess of fifty the annual
license fee shall be One Hundred and Fifty ( $ 150.00 )
Dollars for each such additional store .

230 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 6. Whenever any merchant has paid the license
tax due and shall die or sell any store , stand or place
of business before the expiration of the year for
which the license tax was paid , the authorities by
which such license was originally granted may re-
new the license to the personal representative or
widow of the deceased or purchaser of the store ,
stand or place of business without the payment of
any additional tax for the unexpired period . The
application for the transfer and the granting there-
of shall be in the same way and subject to the same
conditions as the original license except that the
transfer shall be endorsed on the license as original-
ly granted .
§ 7. The administration of this Act is vested in
the State Tax Commission which may prescribe
forms and promulgate rules and regulations needed
for the enforcement thereof . The Commission shall
have power to institute legal proceedings , by the At-
torney General of the State of Kentucky , for the
purpose of enforcing the collection of any tax due
under this Act with penalties thereon , and for the
purpose of enjoining the operation of the business
of the delinquent taxpayer operating a business
made taxable under this act .
$§ 8. Any merchant required by the provisions of
this Act to pay a license tax , who shall fail to pay
said tax at the time required herein , shall be subject
to a penalty equal to twenty per centum of the
amount of tax due and delinquent and , in addition ,
thereto , interest at six per centum per annum .
§ 9. Any merchant who fails to comply with the
provisions of this Act shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor , and upon conviction thereof, shall be sen-
tenced to pay a fine of not less than Twenty -Five
($ 25.00 ) Dollars , nor more than One Thousand ($ 1 ,
000.00 ) Dollars .

CHAPTER 26 231
10. The provisions of this Act are severable ,
and if any of its provisions shall be held unconstitu-
tional , the decisions of the court shall not affect or
impair any of the remaining provisions of this Act .
It is hereby declared as a legislative intent that this
Act would have been adopted had not such unconsti-
tutional provisions been included therein .
§ 11. Chapter 149 of the Acts of the 1930 Gen-
eral Assembly, being Sections 4202a - 1 to 4202a - 12 ,
Kentucky Statutes , 1933 Supplement , is hereby re-
pealed , provided that the repeal of said Chapter 149
shall in no way affect the collection of any tax or the
prosecution of any action seeking to defeat the col-
lection of any tax which has or may accrue under
said Act prior to its repeal , it being the legislative
intent that all taxes accruing prior to the passage of
this Act shall be collected and that all suits or pro-
ceedings seeking to collect or defeat the collection
of any tax under said Act shall be prosecuted to final
judgment unless such tax is paid by the persons ,
firms or corporations seeking to defeat the collec-
tion of such tax , and for the purpose of carrying out
the collection of such taxes all administrative pro-
visions of Chapter 149 of the Acts of the 1930 Gen-
eral Assembly are continued in force until all the
taxes have been collected and paid into the State
Treasury . All other laws or parts of laws in conflict.
herewith are hereby repealed . Provided that this
Act shall not repeal or modify any Act passed at this
Special Session of the General Assembly levying a
tax upon the gross receipts of any business or enter-
prise made taxable by any such Act .
§ 12. The license tax levied herein , shall not be
in lieu of any special state license , occupational or
corporation license tax under the laws now in force ,
or that may hereafter be enacted . Should any retail

232 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
merchant , as defined herein , be liable for and pay
any special state license , occupational or corporation
license tax under the laws now in force , or that may
hereafter be enacted , credit may be taken on the tax
levied in this Act for the amount of such special
license , occupational or corporation license tax so
paid not to exceed the amount of the license tax
levied herein . Provided , that no merchant shall be
given credit on the tax levied herein for any gross
receipts tax that may be levied under any Act passed
by this Special Session of the General Assembly.
§ 13. Whereas, the present State revenues will
not meet the immediate needs for the maintenance
and development of the State government and its
agencies and the State's credit is threatened , an
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
become a law and be effective upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
W. E. ROGERS , Speaker of the House of Representatives .
JAMES H. THOMPSON , Presiding officer of the Senate .
Approved July 3 , 1934 .
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .
CHAPTER 27 .
AN ACT providing that funds realized from certain taxes and
other revenue sources shall be credited to the General Fund
for the expenses of government and further providing for the
crediting of certain taxes to the Road Fund , and excepting
certain other funds to be credited as now or which may here-
åfter be provided by law, and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1 . The aggregate amount of State tax realized
by the assessments made of and levied upon real
estate , all assessments of franchise , shares of
stock , money in hands , notes , bonds , accounts and

CHAPTER 27 233
other credits , whether secured by pledge , mortgage ,
or otherwise , or unsecured , and the tax on bank de-
posits and upon shares of building and loan associa-
tions and upon funds realized from the collection of
inheritance tax levied in Kentucky , beer license
taxes and all license and excise taxes , and the aggre-
gate amount of all other state revenue realized from
every other source whatsoever , excepting operating
receipts and/or revolving fund accounts as defined
in an act entitled " A Financial Code of One Thou-
sand Nine Hundred Thirty -Four " , and further ex-
cepting the gasoline tax , automobile license , motor
bus license , truck license , drivers ' license , and one-
third of the gross receipts tax which by the Gross
Receipts Tax Act is appropriated to the County
Road Fund , and fees collected under Chapter 145 ,
Acts 1934 General Assembly , for the support of the
Utilities Commission , shall be credited to the Gen-
eral Fund for the use of the expenses of government .
§ 2. All funds realized from automobile license ,
motor bus license and gasoline tax , now collected by
law or that may hereafter be imposed by law shall
be credited to the Road Fund for the constructing ,
reconstruction , maintenance and repair of roads and
highways . The County Road Fund shall be distrib-
uted to the various counties of the Commonwealth as
provided by Section 14 of the Gross Receipts Tax
Act of One Thousand Nine Hundred Thirty -Four .
§ 3. All funds realized from truck license and
fees shall be distributed as provided in section two
thousand seven hundred thirty nine g-two , subsec-
tion d , Carroll's 1930 Edition , Kentucky Stat-
utes , and all funds that may be derived from a driv-
ers ' license shall be distributed in the manner set
forth in the act imposing such license . All funds
realized from operating receipts and/or revolving

234 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fund shall be credited as provided in the act entitled
" A Financial Code of One Thousand Nine Hundred
Thirty-Four . '
§ 4. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
to the extent of such conflict are hereby repealed .
§ 5. Whereas , the fiscal year 1934-1935 begins
on July 1 , 1934 , and it is necessary for the Auditor
of Public Accounts and the State Treasurer to set
up their books on said date for the purpose of show-
ing the collection of revenues and to what funds
same shall be credited , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall become effective upon its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved July 10 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 28 .
RESOLUTION providing for the purchase of copies of the Legis-
lative Digest adopted at the Session of one thousand nine
hundred and twelve , and every succeeding session since that
date as the official publication for the General Assembly and
State and certain other public officials .
WHEREAS : It is a matter of importance and convenience that
each Senator and Representative be advised as to the various
and detailed steps of legislation , and
WHEREAS : This General Assembly has for the past fifteen
sessions approved and adopted as its official publication "The
Legislative Digest " , a publication under the editorship and
management of competent and experienced persons , the pur-
pose of which is to fully advise from day to day the officials
and public of the Commonwealth as to matters affecting them
in an intelligent and prompt manner , therefore ,
Be it resolved , by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring:
That the publishers of the Legislative Digest are
hereby directed to furnish daily a copy to each mem-
ber and the clerks of the General Assembly and to
the heads of each State Department during the time

CHAPTER 29 235
of this Special Session . That the publishers are al-
lowed the sum of Three Dollars for each subscrip-
tion for the Session and the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts is directed to draw his warrant upon the
Treasurer for said amount when such service has
been concluded . The total number shall not exceed
( 200 ) two hundred copies which are to be paid for
under this resolution .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 30 .
RESOLUTION providing a contingent fund for the Clerk of the
Senate and Clerk of the House .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That for the purpose of paying such expenses as
the Chief Clerk of the Senate of Kentucky is direct-
ed to incur by the Senate of Kentucky , there
is hereby appropriated out of the General revenues
of the State , as a contingent fund , the sum of Two
Hundred Fifty ( $ 250.00 ) Dollars , and for the pur-
pose of paying the expenses incurred by the Chief
Clerk of the House of Representatives of the Com-
monwealth , under the direction of said House there
is hereby appropriated out of the General Revenues
of the State , as a contingent fund , the sum of Two
Hundred Fifty ( $ 250.00 ) Dollars . Said respective
funds shall be paid out on warrant of the Auditor ,
issued on requisition of the Chief Clerk of the Sen-
ate and of the House of Representatives , and ,
Whereas , it is necessary that the said Clerks
shall have immediate use of said contingent funds ,
Therefore , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist , and this resolution shall take effect from
and after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved June 29 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 32 .
RESOLUTION providing for furnishing Kentucky Directories .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

244 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 1. That the State Librarian is hereby author-
ized and directed to purchase at not exceeding two
dollars per copy five hundred and fifty copies of the
book entitled " Kentucky Directory 1934 " for the
use of the Courts , state and county officers and mem-
bers of the General Assembly . The Librarian will
furnish the members of the General Assembly and
such officers as may be designated by the Presiding
officers of the respective Houses a copy of same
and will furnish the state officials with copies thereof.
and will send a copy thereof to the Clerk of the
County Court of each county in the state to be kept
in his office . The Librarian will furnish to the Li-
brarian of each state and territory of the union a
copy of said book in exchange for similar books from
said states and territories , and a copy to the Libra-
rian of Congress , and deliver to the Secretary of
State fifty copies to exchange .
§ 2. It being necessary that the officers should
have the books herein provided for , an emergency is
declared and this resolution shall take effect upon
its adoption and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 33.
A JOINT AND CONCURRENT RESOLUTION providing for the
payment of money to G. 'W. Lawrence , Mrs. Ollie Brightwell ,
Fletcher Davis , Mrs. Alice Allnutt , A. J. Bauer, Dan Mahoney ,
Joe Rosson , Robert Baker , Dennis Rath , Robert Pulliam and
Forrest Sanford for extra services in caring for the offices in
the State Capitol building during the 1934 session of the
Legislature .
WHEREAS , the aforesaid persons , employees of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , have been required to perform overtime
and extra services because of the regular 1934 session of the
General Assembly ,

CHAPTER 34 245
Now therefore , be it resolved by the House of
Representatives of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Senate of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky concurring therein :
That G. W. Lawrence , A. J. Bauer , Dan Mahoney ,
Joe Rosson , Robert Baker , Dennis Rath , Robert
Pulliam , Forrest Sanford , Mrs. Ollie Brightwell ,
Fletcher Davis and Mrs. Alice Allnut are each here-
by allowed the sum of one dollar ($1.00 ) per day
for each day of the regular 1934 session of the Gen-
eral Assembly , which allowance shall be in addition
to the salaries they now receive for their services .
The appropriation hereby made is to be paid out
of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise ap-
propriated , and the Auditor of Public Accounts is
hereby ordered and directed to draw his warrant
upon the Treasurer to each of the above named
persons for the amount due them as herein provided .
Approved July 2 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 34.
A RESOLUTION providing for an appropriation for the pay-
ment of the expenses of the Legislative Interim Committee ,
appointed under authority of a Resolution of the General As-
sembly , at its regular session of 1934 , and providing for the
payment of the mileage , per diem and expenses of the mem-
bers of said Committee .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund the following sums for
the following purposes :
(a) For the contingent expenses of the Legisla-
tive Interim Committee , appointed under authority

246 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of a Resolution of the General Assembly , at its regu-
lar session of 1934 , including postage , telephone and
telegraph bills , printing and stenographic expenses ,
and assistant secretary , $ 537.00 .
(b ) For the payment of the per diem and mile-
age of the individual members of said Interim Com-
mittee the sum of $ 6,217.20 .
§ 2. The sums appropriated herein shall be paid
to the persons entitled thereto by warrant drawn by
the Auditor of Public Accounts on the Treasurer of
the State , as other claims against the State are paid ,
and a certificate signed by the Chairman of said
Interim Committee , attested by the Secretary there-
of and addressed to the Auditor of Public Accounts ,
showing the name of the person entitled to said
payment , the amount thereof and the purpose for
which said payment is to be paid , shall be sufficient
authority for the Auditor of Public Accounts to
issue a warrant to the person shown to be entitled
thereto .
Whereas , many of the members of said Interim
Committee have been put to large expense in at-
tending the sessions of said Committee and should
be promptly reimbursed , an emergency is declared
to exist and this Resolution shall become effective
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives ,
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate ,
Pursuant to request and direction of a
majority of the members elected to the
Senate .
Approved June 28 , 1934 ,
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 35 247
CHAPTER 35.
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION , declining any interest of the
State of Kentucky in the property of Eleanor Holmes Lind-
say .
WHEREAS , on the seventh day of February one thousand nine
hundred thirty -four , Eleanor Holmes Lindsay died testate , a
resident of Franklin County , Kentucky , and ,
WHEREAS , on the fifth day of March one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -four her will was duly probated by the Franklin
County Court and recorded in the office of the Clerk of the
Franklin County Court in Will Book number seven , page four
hundred and ten , and
WHEREAS , by said will, after certain personal bequests were
made , the said testator , Eleanor Holmes Lindsay , bequeathed
all of her property , consisting of her home located on Wap-
ping and Wilkerson streets in Frankfort , Kentucky , and the
contents therein , and all personal property connected there-
with , to the State of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , said property was encumbeerd by a mortgage unpaid
at the time of the death of the said Eleanor Holmes Lindsay ,
and
WHEREAS , at the regular session of the Kentucky Legislature
for the year one thousand nine hundred thirty -four , sitting at
that time , a bill was introduced providing that the State of
Kentucky should pay said indebtedness and take title to said
property as set out in said will, which the State of Kentucky
in said regular session declined to do , and ,
WHEREAS , a special session of the Kentucky Legislature was
called by the Honorable Ruby Laffoon , Governor of Kentucky ,
to sit at Frankfort , on May nine , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -four, and following , and among matters be called the at-
tention of the Legislature to said will and said property and
requested that they take action on said will , and
WHEREAS , a bill was introduced providing that the State of
Kentucky should take title to said property , being known as
House Bill number twenty -seven , and after due consideration
the said State of Kentucky , through its Legislature , for the
second time declined to accept said bequest and take title to
said property , so now
Therefore , be it resolved by the House of Repre-
sentatives of the State of Kentucky , the Senate con-
curring therein :

248 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
That in obedience to Kentucky Statutes ,
Section two thousand and sixty -seven , the State of
Kentucky does hereby disclaim any interest in and
to any of the property of the said Eleanor Holmes
Lindsay as set out in said will above referred to ,
and declines to accept said bequest and does hereby
release to the heirs of Eleanor Holmes Lindsay any
and all right , title or interest that may have been
created by said will , and the purpose and intent of
said resolution is to remove all cloud from the title
of the heirs of Eleanor Holmes Lindsay and to dis-
claim any interest in said estate and to permit same
to revert to said estate for distribution under the
law , as provided by the Statute of the State of Ken-
tucky .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 36 .
WHEREAS , the President of the United States of America , the
Honorable Franklin Delano Roosevelt , left today on a vacation
cruise , and
WHEREAS , it is understood upon his return from the cruise
that he will dock on the Pacific Coast and make a speaking
tour across the country , and
WHEREAS , the last Democratic President of the United States ,
the late Woodrow 'Wilson , visited Kentucky and delivered an
address at the birthplace of the beloved Abraham Lincoln ,
and
WHEREAS it is further understood that the President of the
United States can , with a little change of his itinerary across
the country , come to Kentucky about the first of August ,
Now , Therefore , be it resolved , that the Gen-
eral Assembly of Kentucky does hereby most re-
spectfully request the President of the United States
to visit Kentucky and to deliver one of his addresses
to the people of these United States at the birth-
place of another great President , and the General

CHAPTER 38 249
Assembly does hereby most respectfully request the
Governor of this Commonwealth to forward an at-
tested copy of this resolution , together with his
personal invitation to the President , and the Gover-
nor of the Commonwealth is hereby authorized ,
upon the acceptance of the invitation , to select a
reception committee of twenty -five , five of whom
shall be selected from the Senate , ten from the House
of Representatives , and ten from the citizens of the
State at large.
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 37.
WHEREAS , L. J. Spencer , beloved member of the House of
Representatives , has passed to the great beyond , and
WHEREAS , there was due per diem to him as a member of the
House of Representatives at the time of his death , and
WHEREAS , only a few legislative days of the Special Session
remain , therefore ,
Be it resolved , by the House of Representatives ,
the Senate concurring therein :
That the Clerk of the House issue a voucher on
day of adjournment for the payment of all the per
diem which the said Spencer would have collected
had he lived and that the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts issue warrant payable to his estate for the
sum certified in the voucher .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 38 .
BE IT RESOLVED by the Senate , The House of Representatives
concurring herein :
THAT WHEREAS , Hon . Fred C. Van Hoose , a member of the
General Assembly of the State of Kentucky has been , and is
now seriously ill at his home in Johnson county , Kentucky ,

250 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and by reason of said illness is unable to attend in person , at
special session of the said General Assembly of nineteen hun-
dred thirty -four , and
WHEREAS , through no fault of his own , the said Van Hoose is
deprived of his per diem as a Senator through his inability to
attend said special session , now therefore ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring herein :
That the said Fred C. Van Hoose shall be paid his
per diem as a member of the Senate at the rate of
Ten ( 10 ) Dollars per day and stationery allow-
ance for the entire Special Session as though he had
been personally present at each of said sessions dur-
ing said special session , and the Clerk of the Senate
is hereby directed to issue his order on the Auditor
for the State of Kentucky for the said sum that will
be due when the total number of legislative days of
the special session shall have been determined , said
order to be payable to the said Fred C. Van Hoose ,
above mentioned , and the Auditor will issue his war-
rant on the Treasurer for the amount thereof.
Approved June 11 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 39 .
Be it resolved by the House of Representatives ,
the Senate concurring therein :
That when this the nineteen hundred and thirty-
four Special Session adourns July second , nineteen
hundred and thirty -four , that they adjourn sine die .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
